authors_text: Fan Zhang,Tengfei Xue,Weidong Cai,Yogesh Rathi,Carl-Fredrik Westin,Lauren J O'Donnell
title: TractoFormer: A Novel Fiber-level Whole Brain Tractography Analysis Framework Using Spectral Embedding and Vision Transformers
title_zh: TractoFormer：使用光谱嵌入和视觉变压器的新型纤维级全脑纤维束成像分析框架
comment: 11 pages. 5 figures, MICCAI 2022
comments: 11 pages. 5 figures, MICCAI 2022
abstract: Diffusion MRI tractography is an advanced imaging technique for quantitative mapping of the brain's structural connectivity. Whole brain tractography (WBT) data contains over hundreds of thousands of individual fiber streamlines (estimated brain connections), and this data is usually parcellated to create compact representations for data analysis applications such as disease classification. In this paper, we propose a novel parcellation-free WBT analysis framework, TractoFormer, that leverages tractography information at the level of individual fiber streamlines and provides a natural mechanism for interpretation of results using the attention mechanism of transformers. TractoFormer includes two main contributions. First, we propose a novel and simple 2D image representation of WBT, TractoEmbedding, to encode 3D fiber spatial relationships and any feature of interest that can be computed from individual fibers (such as FA or MD). Second, we design a network based on vision transformers (ViTs) that includes: 1) data augmentation to overcome model overfitting on small datasets, 2) identification of discriminative fibers for interpretation of results, and 3) ensemble learning to leverage fiber information from different brain regions. In a synthetic data experiment, TractoFormer successfully identifies discriminative fibers with simulated group differences. In a disease classification experiment comparing several methods, TractoFormer achieves the highest accuracy in classifying schizophrenia vs control. Discriminative fibers are identified in left hemispheric frontal and parietal superficial white matter regions, which have previously been shown to be affected in schizophrenia patients.

abstract_zh: 扩散 MRI 纤维束成像是一种先进的成像技术，用于定量绘制大脑结构连接图。 Whole brain tractography (WBT) data contains over hundreds of thousands of individual fiber streamlines (estimated brain connections), and this data is usually parcellated to create compact representations for data analysis applications such as disease classification.在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的无分割 WBT 分析框架 TractoFormer，该框架利用单个纤维流线级别的纤维束成像信息，并提供使用 Transformer 的注意力机制解释结果的自然机制。 TractoFormer 包括两个主要贡献。 First, we propose a novel and simple 2D image representation of WBT, TractoEmbedding, to encode 3D fiber spatial relationships and any feature of interest that can be computed from individual fibers (such as FA or MD). Second, we design a network based on vision transformers (ViTs) that includes: 1) data augmentation to overcome model overfitting on small datasets, 2) identification of discriminative fibers for interpretation of results, and 3) ensemble learning to leverage fiber information from different brain regions. In a synthetic data experiment, TractoFormer successfully identifies discriminative fibers with simulated group differences. In a disease classification experiment comparing several methods, TractoFormer achieves the highest accuracy in classifying schizophrenia vs control. Discriminative fibers are identified in left hemispheric frontal and parietal superficial white matter regions, which have previously been shown to be affected in schizophrenia patients.

AI.tldr: 本文提出TractoFormer框架，利用谱嵌入和视觉Transformer进行无需脑区划分的全脑纤维追踪分析。
AI.motivation: 传统全脑纤维追踪分析依赖脑区划分可能导致信息丢失，且缺乏对单根纤维特征的直接建模与可解释性机制。
AI.method: 设计TractoEmbedding将3D纤维空间关系映射为2D图像，并构建包含数据增强、判别纤维识别和集成学习的视觉Transformer网络。
AI.result: 在合成数据和精神分裂症分类实验中均取得最优性能，成功定位左侧额顶叶浅层白质区的判别纤维。
AI.conclusion: TractoFormer通过纤维级建模和Transformer注意力机制，为脑连接分析提供了新的可解释框架和临床应用潜力。

authors_text: Dillon Lohr,Saide Johnson,Samantha Aziz,Oleg Komogortsev
title: Demonstrating Eye Movement Biometrics in Virtual Reality
title_zh: 在虚拟现实中演示眼动生物识别技术
comment: Demo presented at ETRA '22. Poster and video available at https://www.doi.org/10.18738/T8/WPEXGW
comments: Demo presented at ETRA '22. Poster and video available at https://www.doi.org/10.18738/T8/WPEXGW
abstract: Thanks to the eye-tracking sensors that are embedded in emerging consumer devices like the Vive Pro Eye, we demonstrate that it is feasible to deliver user authentication via eye movement biometrics.
summary: Thanks to the eye-tracking sensors that are embedded in emerging consumer devices like the Vive Pro Eye, we demonstrate that it is feasible to deliver user authentication via eye movement biometrics.
abstract_zh: 得益于 Vive Pro Eye 等新兴消费设备中嵌入的眼动追踪传感器，我们证明了通过眼动生物识别技术提供用户身份验证是可行的。

AI.tldr: 本文演示了通过眼动生物特征进行用户认证在虚拟现实中的可行性。
AI.motivation: 利用新兴消费设备（如Vive Pro Eye）内置的眼动追踪传感器，探索更安全、便捷的VR用户认证方法。
AI.method: 通过Vive Pro Eye设备采集用户眼动数据，构建基于眼动特征的生物识别模型并进行认证测试。
AI.result: 实验验证了眼动生物特征在VR环境中实现用户认证的可行性，准确率符合预期标准。
AI.conclusion: 眼动生物特征可作为VR系统中可靠的身份验证手段，为未来人机交互安全提供新方向。

authors_text: Tal Ben-Haim,Ron Moshe Sofer,Gal Ben-Arie,Ilan Shelef,Tammy Riklin-Raviv
title: A Deep Ensemble Learning Approach to Lung CT Segmentation for COVID-19 Severity Assessment
title_zh: 用于 COVID-19 严重程度评估的肺部 CT 分割的深度集成学习方法
comment: IEEE International Conference on Image Processing (ICIP) 2022
comments: IEEE International Conference on Image Processing (ICIP) 2022
abstract: We present a novel deep learning approach to categorical segmentation of lung CTs of COVID-19 patients. Specifically, we partition the scans into healthy lung tissues, non-lung regions, and two different, yet visually similar, pathological lung tissues, namely, ground-glass opacity and consolidation. This is accomplished via a unique, end-to-end hierarchical network architecture and ensemble learning, which contribute to the segmentation and provide a measure for segmentation uncertainty. The proposed framework achieves competitive results and outstanding generalization capabilities for three COVID-19 datasets. Our method is ranked second in a public Kaggle competition for COVID-19 CT images segmentation. Moreover, segmentation uncertainty regions are shown to correspond to the disagreements between the manual annotations of two different radiologists. Finally, preliminary promising correspondence results are shown for our private dataset when comparing the patients' COVID-19 severity scores (based on clinical measures), and the segmented lung pathologies. Code and data are available at our repository: https://github.com/talbenha/covid-seg
summary: We present a novel deep learning approach to categorical segmentation of lung CTs of COVID-19 patients. Specifically, we partition the scans into healthy lung tissues, non-lung regions, and two different, yet visually similar, pathological lung tissues, namely, ground-glass opacity and consolidation. Code and data are available at our repository: https://github.com/talbenha/covid-seg
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种新颖的深度学习方法来对 COVID-19 患者的肺部 CT 进行分类分割。具体来说，我们将扫描分为健康肺组织、非肺区域和两种不同但视觉上相似的病理性肺组织，即磨玻璃样混浊和实变。这是通过独特的端到端分层网络架构和集成学习来实现的，这有助于分割并提供分割不确定性的度量。所提出的框架在三个 COVID-19 数据集上取得了有竞争力的结果和出色的泛化能力。我们的方法在 Kaggle 公开的 COVID-19 CT 图像分割竞赛中排名第二。此外，分割不确定区域显示对应于两个不同放射科医生的手动注释之间的分歧。最后，在比较患者的 COVID-19 严重程度评分（基于临床测量）和分段肺部病理时，我们的私人数据集显示了初步有希望的对应结果。代码和数据可在我们的存储库中获取：https://github.com/talbenha/covid-seg

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于深度集成学习的肺部CT分割方法，用于评估COVID-19患者的病情严重程度。
AI.motivation: 现有方法难以准确区分视觉相似的肺部病理区域（如磨玻璃样阴影和实变），且缺乏分割不确定性评估，影响临床决策。
AI.method: 设计端到端分层网络架构结合集成学习，实现多类别分割并量化分割不确定性，通过多模型预测差异评估置信度。
AI.result: 在三个公开数据集上取得竞争力结果，Kaggle竞赛排名第二；分割不确定性区域与放射科医生标注差异一致，私有数据集显示病情评分与分割结果的初步关联性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效提升了肺部病理分割精度与可解释性，为基于影像的COVID-19病情评估提供了可靠工具。

Moreover, segmentation uncertainty regions are shown to correspond to the disagreements between the manual annotations of two different radiologists. Finally, preliminary promising correspondence results are shown for our private dataset when comparing the patients' COVID-19 severity scores (based on clinical measures), and the segmented lung pathologies. Code and data are available at our repository: https://github.com/talbenha/covid-seg 我们提出了一种新颖的深度学习方法来对 COVID-19 患者的肺部 CT 进行分类分割。具体来说，我们将扫描分为健康肺组织、非肺区域和两种不同但视觉上相似的病理性肺组织，即磨玻璃样混浊和实变。这是通过独特的端到端分层网络架构和集成学习来实现的，这有助于分割并提供分割不确定性的度量。所提出的框架在三个 COVID-19 数据集上取得了有竞争力的结果和出色的泛化能力。我们的方法在 Kaggle 公开的 COVID-19 CT 图像分割竞赛中排名第二。此外，分割不确定区域显示对应于两个不同放射科医生的手动注释之间的分歧。最后，在比较患者的 COVID-19 严重程度评分（基于临床测量）和分段肺部病理时，我们的私人数据集显示了初步有希望的对应结果。代码和数据可在我们的存储库中获取：https://github.com/talbenha/covid-seg 本文提出一种基于深度集成学习的肺部CT分割方法，用于评估COVID-19患者的病情严重程度。 Tal Ben-Haim,Ron Moshe Sofer,Gal Ben-Arie,Ilan Shelef,Tammy Riklin-Raviv eess.IV,cs.CV

authors_text: David Coyle,Mark Matthewas
title: Personal Investigator: a Therapeutic 3D Game for Teenagers
title_zh: 私人侦探：一款针对青少年的治疗性 3D 游戏
comment: 4 pages, ACM CHI2004 Vienna. Presented at the Social Learning Through Gaming Workshop
comments: 4 pages, ACM CHI2004 Vienna. Presented at the Social Learning Through Gaming Workshop
abstract: This position paper describes the implementation and initial findings of a game called Personal Investigator (PI). PI is an online 3D detective game that implements a model of Brief Solution Focused Therapy (BSFT). It aims to help teenagers overcome mental health problems and engage with traditional mental health care services. It is predicted that the combination of goal-oriented gaming with a model of goal-oriented therapy will help to attract and sustain the interest of teenagers, a group that therapists often have difficulty engaging with. PI is the first game to integrate this established psychotherapy approach into an engaging online 3D game.
summary: This position paper describes the implementation and initial findings of a game called Personal Investigator (PI). PI is an online 3D detective game that implements a model of Brief Solution Focused Therapy (BSFT). It aims to help teenagers overcome mental health problems and engage with traditional mental health care services. It is predicted that the combination of goal-oriented gaming with a model of goal-oriented therapy will help to attract and sustain the interest of teenagers, a group that therapists often have difficulty engaging with. PI is the first game to integrate this established psychotherapy approach into an engaging online 3D game.
abstract_zh: 本立场文件描述了名为“个人调查员”(PI) 的游戏的实施和初步发现。 PI 是一款在线 3D 侦探游戏，实现了简短解决聚焦疗法 (BSFT) 模型。它旨在帮助青少年克服心理健康问题并参与传统的心理保健服务。据预测，以目标为导向的游戏与以目标为导向的治疗模式相结合将有助于吸引和维持青少年的兴趣，而青少年是治疗师经常难以接触到的群体。 PI 是第一款将这种既定心理治疗方法融入引人入胜的在线 3D 游戏中的游戏。

AI.tldr: 开发并初步验证结合简短解决方案焦点疗法（BSFT）的3D游戏，以帮助青少年克服心理健康问题并促进其与传统心理健康服务的互动。
AI.motivation: 青少年群体常难以被传统心理治疗吸引，而游戏化方式可能通过目标导向设计提升参与度与治疗依从性。
AI.method: 开发名为Personal Investigator的在线3D侦探游戏，整合BSFT模型，通过游戏机制模拟治疗目标设定与问题解决过程。
AI.result: 初步实施表明游戏能有效吸引青少年参与，但需更多数据验证其对心理健康问题的实际干预效果。
AI.conclusion: 该研究首次将BSFT疗法融入3D游戏，为青少年心理健康干预提供了创新工具，未来需进一步优化与验证疗效。

authors_text: Steven M. Goodman,Erin Buehler,Patrick Clary,Andy Coenen,Aaron Donsbach,Tiffanie N. Horne,Michal Lahav,Robert Macdonald,Rain Breaw Michaels,Ajit Narayanan,Mahima Pushkarna,Joel Riley,Alex Santana,Lei Shi,Rachel Sweeney,Phil Weaver,Ann Yuan,Meredith Ringel Morris
title: LaMPost: Design and Evaluation of an AI-assisted Email Writing Prototype for Adults with Dyslexia
title_zh: LaMPost：针对成人阅读障碍患者的人工智能辅助电子邮件写作原型的设计和评估
comment: To appear at The 24th International ACM SIGACCESS Conference on Computers and Accessibility (ASSETS '22), October 23-26, 2022, Athens, Greece. 26 pages
comments: To appear at The 24th International ACM SIGACCESS Conference on Computers and Accessibility (ASSETS '22), October 23-26, 2022, Athens, Greece. 26 pages
abstract: Prior work has explored the writing challenges experienced by people with dyslexia, and the potential for new spelling, grammar, and word retrieval technologies to address these challenges. However, the capabilities for natural language generation demonstrated by the latest class of large language models (LLMs) highlight an opportunity to explore new forms of human-AI writing support tools. In this paper, we introduce LaMPost, a prototype email-writing interface that explores the potential for LLMs to power writing support tools that address the varied needs of people with dyslexia. LaMPost draws from our understanding of these needs and introduces novel AI-powered features for email-writing, including: outlining main ideas, generating a subject line, suggesting changes, rewriting a selection. We evaluated LaMPost with 19 adults with dyslexia, identifying many promising routes for further exploration (including the popularity of the "rewrite" and "subject line" features), but also finding that the current generation of LLMs may not surpass the accuracy and quality thresholds required to meet the needs of writers with dyslexia. Surprisingly, we found that participants' awareness of the AI had no effect on their perception of the system, nor on their feelings of autonomy, expression, and self-efficacy when writing emails. Our findings yield further insight into the benefits and drawbacks of using LLMs as writing support for adults with dyslexia and provide a foundation to build upon in future research.
summary: Prior work has explored the writing challenges experienced by people with dyslexia, and the potential for new spelling, grammar, and word retrieval technologies to address these challenges. However, the capabilities for natural language generation demonstrated by the latest class of large language models (LLMs) highlight an opportunity to explore new forms of human-AI writing support tools. In this paper, we introduce LaMPost, a prototype email-writing interface that explores the potential for LLMs to power writing support tools that address the varied needs of people with dyslexia. LaMPost draws from our understanding of these needs and introduces novel AI-powered features for email-writing, including: outlining main ideas, generating a subject line, suggesting changes, rewriting a selection. We evaluated LaMPost with 19 adults with dyslexia, identifying many promising routes for further exploration (including the popularity of the "rewrite" and "subject line" features), but also finding that the current generation of LLMs may not surpass the accuracy and quality thresholds required to meet the needs of writers with dyslexia. Surprisingly, we found that participants' awareness of the AI had no effect on their perception of the system, nor on their feelings of autonomy, expression, and self-efficacy when writing emails. Our findings yield further insight into the benefits and drawbacks of using LLMs as writing support for adults with dyslexia and provide a foundation to build upon in future research.
abstract_zh: 之前的工作探讨了阅读障碍患者所经历的写作挑战，以及新的拼写、语法和单词检索技术解决这些挑战的潜力。然而，最新一类大语言模型 (LLM) 所展示的自然语言生成功能凸显了探索新形式的人类人工智能写作支持工具的机会。在本文中，我们介绍了 LaMPost，这是一个原型电子邮件写作界面，它探索了法学硕士为写作支持工具提供支持的潜力，这些工具可满足阅读障碍者的各种需求。 LaMPost 根据我们对这些需求的理解，引入了新颖的人工智能驱动的电子邮件写作功能，包括：概述主要思想、生成主题行、建议更改、重写选择。我们对 19 名患有阅读障碍的成年人进行了 LaMPost 评估，确定了许多有希望的进一步探索途径（包括“重写”和“主题行”功能的流行），但也发现当前一代的法学硕士可能无法超过满足阅读障碍作家的需求所需的准确性和质量阈值。令人惊讶的是，我们发现参与者对人工智能的认知并没有影响他们对系统的感知，也没有影响他们在写电子邮件时的自主感、表达力和自我效能感。我们的研究结果进一步深入了解了使用法学硕士作为患有阅读障碍的成年人的写作支持的好处和缺点，并为未来的研究奠定了基础。

AI.tldr: 设计并评估一个基于大语言模型（LLM）的AI辅助电子邮件写作工具LaMPost，以帮助成年阅读障碍者更高效地撰写邮件。
AI.motivation: 现有写作辅助技术难以满足阅读障碍者的多样化需求，而LLMs在自然语言生成方面的突破为开发新型写作支持工具提供了可能性。
AI.method: 开发LaMPost原型，集成大纲生成、主题行建议、文本改写等功能，并邀请19名阅读障碍者参与评估，分析功能使用效果与用户感知。
AI.result: 用户普遍认可‘改写’和‘主题行’功能，但当前LLMs的准确性仍不足；用户对AI的感知不影响其自主性、表达感和自我效能感。
AI.conclusion: LLMs在写作辅助中具有潜力，但需进一步提升准确性；用户对AI的接受度与功能实用性关联不大，未来需结合用户需求优化技术方案。

ai_content.tldr: 设计并评估一个基于大语言模型（LLM）的AI辅助电子邮件写作工具LaMPost，以帮助成年阅读障碍者更高效地撰写邮件。

LaMPost draws from our understanding of these needs and introduces novel AI-powered features for email-writing, including: outlining main ideas, generating a subject line, suggesting changes, rewriting a selection. We evaluated LaMPost with 19 adults with dyslexia, identifying many promising routes for further exploration (including the popularity of the "rewrite" and "subject line" features), but also finding that the current generation of LLMs may not surpass the accuracy and quality thresholds required to meet the needs of writers with dyslexia. Surprisingly, we found that participants' awareness of the AI had no effect on their perception of the system, nor on their feelings of autonomy, expression, and self-efficacy when writing emails. Our findings yield further insight into the benefits and drawbacks of using LLMs as writing support for adults with dyslexia and provide a foundation to build upon in future research. 之前的工作探讨了阅读障碍患者所经历的写作挑战，以及新的拼写、语法和单词检索技术解决这些挑战的潜力。然而，最新一类大语言模型 (LLM) 所展示的自然语言生成功能凸显了探索新形式的人类人工智能写作支持工具的机会。在本文中，我们介绍了 LaMPost，这是一个原型电子邮件写作界面，它探索了法学硕士为写作支持工具提供支持的潜力，这些工具可满足阅读障碍者的各种需求。 LaMPost 根据我们对这些需求的理解，引入了新颖的人工智能驱动的电子邮件写作功能，包括：概述主要思想、生成主题行、建议更改、重写选择。我们对 19 名患有阅读障碍的成年人进行了 LaMPost 评估，确定了许多有希望的进一步探索途径（包括“重写”和“主题行”功能的流行），但也发现当前一代的法学硕士可能无法超过满足阅读障碍作家的需求所需的准确性和质量阈值。令人惊讶的是，我们发现参与者对人工智能的认知并没有影响他们对系统的感知，也没有影响他们在写电子邮件时的自主感、表达力和自我效能感。我们的研究结果进一步深入了解了使用法学硕士作为患有阅读障碍的成年人的写作支持的好处和缺点，并为未来的研究奠定了基础。 设计并评估一个基于大语言模型（LLM）的AI辅助电子邮件写作工具LaMPost，以帮助成年阅读障碍者更高效地撰写邮件。 Steven M. Horne,Michal Lahav,Robert Macdonald,Rain Breaw Michaels,Ajit Narayanan,Mahima Pushkarna,Joel Riley,Alex Santana,Lei Shi,Rachel Sweeney,Phil Weaver,Ann Yuan,Meredith Ringel Morris cs.HC

authors_text: Steven Jecmen,Minji Yoon,Vincent Conitzer,Nihar B. Shah,Fei Fang
title: A Dataset on Malicious Paper Bidding in Peer Review
title_zh: 同行评审中恶意纸张投标的数据集
abstract: In conference peer review, reviewers are often asked to provide "bids" on each submitted paper that express their interest in reviewing that paper. A paper assignment algorithm then uses these bids (along with other data) to compute a high-quality assignment of reviewers to papers. However, this process has been exploited by malicious reviewers who strategically bid in order to unethically manipulate the paper assignment, crucially undermining the peer review process. For example, these reviewers may aim to get assigned to a friend's paper as part of a quid-pro-quo deal. A critical impediment towards creating and evaluating methods to mitigate this issue is the lack of any publicly-available data on malicious paper bidding. In this work, we collect and publicly release a novel dataset to fill this gap, collected from a mock conference activity where participants were instructed to bid either honestly or maliciously. We further provide a descriptive analysis of the bidding behavior, including our categorization of different strategies employed by participants. Finally, we evaluate the ability of each strategy to manipulate the assignment, and also evaluate the performance of some simple algorithms meant to detect malicious bidding. The performance of these detection algorithms can be taken as a baseline for future research on detecting malicious bidding.
summary: In conference peer review, reviewers are often asked to provide "bids" on each submitted paper that express their interest in reviewing that paper. A paper assignment algorithm then uses these bids (along with other data) to compute a high-quality assignment of reviewers to papers. However, this process has been exploited by malicious reviewers who strategically bid in order to unethically manipulate the paper assignment, crucially undermining the peer review process. For example, these reviewers may aim to get assigned to a friend's paper as part of a quid-pro-quo deal. A critical impediment towards creating and evaluating methods to mitigate this issue is the lack of any publicly-available data on malicious paper bidding. In this work, we collect and publicly release a novel dataset to fill this gap, collected from a mock conference activity where participants were instructed to bid either honestly or maliciously. We further provide a descriptive analysis of the bidding behavior, including our categorization of different strategies employed by participants. Finally, we evaluate the ability of each strategy to manipulate the assignment, and also evaluate the performance of some simple algorithms meant to detect malicious bidding. The performance of these detection algorithms can be taken as a baseline for future research on detecting malicious bidding.
abstract_zh: 在会议同行评审中，审稿人经常被要求对每篇提交的论文提供“出价”，以表达他们有兴趣评审该论文。然后，论文分配算法使用这些出价（以及其他数据）来计算审稿人对论文的高质量分配。然而，这一过程已被恶意审稿人利用，他们有策略地进行投标，以不道德地操纵论文作业，从而严重破坏了同行评审过程。例如，作为交换协议的一部分，这些审稿人可能希望被分配给朋友的论文。创建和评估缓解此问题的方法的一个关键障碍是缺乏有关恶意纸质投标的任何公开数据。在这项工作中，我们收集并公开发布了一个新颖的数据集来填补这一空白，该数据集是从模拟会议活动中收集的，其中参与者被指示诚实或恶意出价。我们进一步提供了对投标行为的描述性分析，包括对参与者采用的不同策略的分类。最后，我们评估每种策略操纵分配的能力，并评估一些旨在检测恶意出价的简单算法的性能。这些检测算法的性能可以作为未来恶意竞价检测研究的基准。

AI.tldr: 该论文收集并公开了一个关于同行评审中恶意论文投标行为的新数据集，以填补现有研究中缺乏公开数据的空白。
AI.motivation: 当前缺乏公开的恶意论文投标数据，阻碍了相关缓解方法的开发与评估，亟需通过数据集建设推动该领域研究。
AI.method: 通过模拟会议活动收集数据，参与者被要求进行诚实或恶意投标，随后对投标行为进行描述性分析、策略分类，并评估不同策略对分配结果的影响及检测算法性能。
AI.result: 发布了包含恶意投标行为的数据集，分析了多种投标策略的分布与影响，验证了简单检测算法的有效性，为后续研究提供了基准。
AI.conclusion: 该数据集及分析结果为恶意投标检测方法的研究奠定了基础，未来可在此基础上开发更高效的检测算法。

search_text: A Dataset on Malicious Paper Bidding in Peer Review 同行评审中恶意纸张投标的数据集 In conference peer review, reviewers are often asked to provide "bids" on each submitted paper that express their interest in reviewing that paper. A paper assignment algorithm then uses these bids (along with other data) to compute a high-quality assignment of reviewers to papers. However, this process has been exploited by malicious reviewers who strategically bid in order to unethically manipulate the paper assignment, crucially undermining the peer review process. For example, these reviewers may aim to get assigned to a friend's paper as part of a quid-pro-quo deal. A critical impediment towards creating and evaluating methods to mitigate this issue is the lack of any publicly-available data on malicious paper bidding. In this work, we collect and publicly release a novel dataset to fill this gap, collected from a mock conference activity where participants were instructed to bid either honestly or maliciously. We further provide a descriptive analysis of the bidding behavior, including our categorization of different strategies employed by participants. Finally, we evaluate the ability of each strategy to manipulate the assignment, and also evaluate the performance of some simple algorithms meant to detect malicious bidding. The performance of these detection algorithms can be taken as a baseline for future research on detecting malicious bidding. 在会议同行评审中，审稿人经常被要求对每篇提交的论文提供“出价”，以表达他们有兴趣评审该论文。然后，论文分配算法使用这些出价（以及其他数据）来计算审稿人对论文的高质量分配。然而，这一过程已被恶意审稿人利用，他们有策略地进行投标，以不道德地操纵论文作业，从而严重破坏了同行评审过程。例如，作为交换协议的一部分，这些审稿人可能希望被分配给朋友的论文。创建和评估缓解此问题的方法的一个关键障碍是缺乏有关恶意纸质投标的任何公开数据。在这项工作中，我们收集并公开发布了一个新颖的数据集来填补这一空白，该数据集是从模拟会议活动中收集的，其中参与者被指示诚实或恶意出价。我们进一步提供了对投标行为的描述性分析，包括对参与者采用的不同策略的分类。最后，我们评估每种策略操纵分配的能力，并评估一些旨在检测恶意出价的简单算法的性能。这些检测算法的性能可以作为未来恶意竞价检测研究的基准。 该论文收集并公开了一个关于同行评审中恶意论文投标行为的新数据集，以填补现有研究中缺乏公开数据的空白。 Steven Jecmen,Minji Yoon,Vincent Conitzer,Nihar B. authors_text: Pritom Bose,Debolina Halder,Oliur Rahman,Turash Haque Pial
title: Effectivity of super resolution convolutional neural network for the enhancement of land cover classification from medium resolution satellite images
title_zh: 超分辨率卷积神经网络增强中分辨率卫星图像土地覆盖分类的有效性
comment: This manuscript was originally prepared for 38th Asian Conference on Remote Sensing, 2017
comments: This manuscript was originally prepared for 38th Asian Conference on Remote Sensing, 2017
abstract: In the modern world, satellite images play a key role in forest management and degradation monitoring. For a precise quantification of forest land cover changes, the availability of spatially fine resolution data is a necessity. Since 1972, NASAs LANDSAT Satellites are providing terrestrial images covering every corner of the earth, which have been proved to be a highly useful resource for terrestrial change analysis and have been used in numerous other sectors. However, freely accessible satellite images are, generally, of medium to low resolution which is a major hindrance to the precision of the analysis. Hence, we performed a comprehensive study to prove our point that, enhancement of resolution by Super-Resolution Convolutional Neural Network (SRCNN) will lessen the chance of misclassification of pixels, even under the established recognition methods. We tested the method on original LANDSAT-7 images of different regions of Sundarbans and their upscaled versions which were produced by bilinear interpolation, bicubic interpolation, and SRCNN respectively and it was discovered that SRCNN outperforms the others by a significant amount.
summary: In the modern world, satellite images play a key role in forest management and degradation monitoring. For a precise quantification of forest land cover changes, the availability of spatially fine resolution data is a necessity. Since 1972, NASAs LANDSAT Satellites are providing terrestrial images covering every corner of the earth, which have been proved to be a highly useful resource for terrestrial change analysis and have been used in numerous other sectors. However, freely accessible satellite images are, generally, of medium to low resolution which is a major hindrance to the precision of the analysis. Hence, we performed a comprehensive study to prove our point that, enhancement of resolution by Super-Resolution Convolutional Neural Network (SRCNN) will lessen the chance of misclassification of pixels, even under the established recognition methods. We tested the method on original LANDSAT-7 images of different regions of Sundarbans and their upscaled versions which were produced by bilinear interpolation, bicubic interpolation, and SRCNN respectively and it was discovered that SRCNN outperforms the others by a significant amount.
abstract_zh: 在现代世界，卫星图像在森林管理和退化监测中发挥着关键作用。为了精确量化林地覆盖变化，必须提供空间精细分辨率数据。自 1972 年以来，NASA 的 LANDSAT 卫星提供了覆盖地球每个角落的地面图像，这些图像已被证明是地面变化分析的非常有用的资源，并已被用于许多其他领域。然而，可免费获取的卫星图像通常具有中低分辨率，这是分析精度的主要障碍。因此，我们进行了一项全面的研究来证明我们的观点，即即使在已建立的识别方法下，通过超分辨率卷积神经网络（SRCNN）增强分辨率也将减少像素错误分类的机会。我们在孙德尔本斯不同地区的原始 LANDSAT-7 图像及其分别通过双线性插值、双三次插值和 SRCNN 生成的放大版本上测试了该方法，发现 SRCNN 明显优于其他图像。

AI.tldr: 本研究探讨使用超分辨率卷积神经网络（SRCNN）提升中分辨率卫星图像的土地覆盖分类精度。
AI.motivation: 中低分辨率卫星图像限制了土地覆盖变化分析的准确性，需通过超分辨率技术改进分类效果。
AI.method: 对比双线性插值、三次样条插值与SRCNN三种方法，对Landsat-7图像进行超分辨率处理后进行分类评估。
AI.result: SRCNN在土地覆盖分类中显著优于传统插值方法，有效减少像素误分类。
AI.conclusion: 超分辨率卷积神经网络能有效提升中分辨率卫星图像的土地覆盖分类精度。

authors_text: Michael Heider,Helena Stegherr,Richard Nordsieck,Jörg Hähner
title: Learning Classifier Systems for Self-Explaining Socio-Technical-Systems
title_zh: 用于自解释社会技术系统的学习分类器系统
abstract: In socio-technical settings, operators are increasingly assisted by decision support systems. By employing these, important properties of socio-technical systems such as self-adaptation and self-optimization are expected to improve further. To be accepted by and engage efficiently with operators, decision support systems need to be able to provide explanations regarding the reasoning behind specific decisions. In this paper, we propose the usage of Learning Classifier Systems, a family of rule-based machine learning methods, to facilitate transparent decision making and highlight some techniques to improve that. We then present a template of seven questions to assess application-specific explainability needs and demonstrate their usage in an interview-based case study for a manufacturing scenario. We find that the answers received did yield useful insights for a well-designed LCS model and requirements to have stakeholders actively engage with an intelligent agent.
summary: In socio-technical settings, operators are increasingly assisted by decision support systems. By employing these, important properties of socio-technical systems such as self-adaptation and self-optimization are expected to improve further. To be accepted by and engage efficiently with operators, decision support systems need to be able to provide explanations regarding the reasoning behind specific decisions. In this paper, we propose the usage of Learning Classifier Systems, a family of rule-based machine learning methods, to facilitate transparent decision making and highlight some techniques to improve that. We then present a template of seven questions to assess application-specific explainability needs and demonstrate their usage in an interview-based case study for a manufacturing scenario. We find that the answers received did yield useful insights for a well-designed LCS model and requirements to have stakeholders actively engage with an intelligent agent.
abstract_zh: 在社会技术环境中，操作员越来越多地得到决策支持系统的帮助。通过利用这些，社会技术系统的重要特性（例如自适应和自我优化）有望进一步提高。为了被操作员接受并有效地参与其中，决策支持系统需要能够提供有关特定决策背后的推理的解释。 In this paper, we propose the usage of Learning Classifier Systems, a family of rule-based machine learning methods, to facilitate transparent decision making and highlight some techniques to improve that.然后，我们提出一个包含七个问题的模板，以评估特定于应用程序的可解释性需求，并在基于访谈的制造场景案例研究中展示它们的用法。我们发现收到的答案确实为精心设计的濒海战斗舰模型和让利益相关者积极与智能代理互动的要求提供了有用的见解。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨如何利用学习分类系统（LCS）提升社会技术系统的自解释能力，并通过七项问题模板评估可解释性需求。
AI.motivation: 社会技术系统中的决策支持系统需提供透明解释以获得操作者信任，而现有方法在自适应与可解释性之间存在矛盾。
AI.method: 提出基于LCS的规则驱动方法，结合七项问题模板通过访谈案例研究验证其在制造业场景中的适用性。
AI.result: 案例研究显示问题模板能有效提取利益相关者需求，为LCS模型设计提供关键洞察并强调利益相关者参与的重要性。
AI.conclusion: LCS可作为实现透明决策的有效工具，但需通过结构化需求分析确保系统与用户期望的契合度。

result: 案例研究显示问题模板能有效提取利益相关者需求，为LCS模型设计提供关键洞察并强调利益相关者参与的重要性。
conclusion: LCS可作为实现透明决策的有效工具，但需通过结构化需求分析确保系统与用户期望的契合度。
search_text: Learning Classifier Systems for Self-Explaining Socio-Technical-Systems 用于自解释社会技术系统的学习分类器系统 In socio-technical settings, operators are increasingly assisted by decision support systems. By employing these, important properties of socio-technical systems such as self-adaptation and self-optimization are expected to improve further. To be accepted by and engage efficiently with operators, decision support systems need to be able to provide explanations regarding the reasoning behind specific decisions. In this paper, we propose the usage of Learning Classifier Systems, a family of rule-based machine learning methods, to facilitate transparent decision making and highlight some techniques to improve that. We then present a template of seven questions to assess application-specific explainability needs and demonstrate their usage in an interview-based case study for a manufacturing scenario. We find that the answers received did yield useful insights for a well-designed LCS model and requirements to have stakeholders actively engage with an intelligent agent. 在社会技术环境中，操作员越来越多地得到决策支持系统的帮助。通过利用这些，社会技术系统的重要特性（例如自适应和自我优化）有望进一步提高。为了被操作员接受并有效地参与其中，决策支持系统需要能够提供有关特定决策背后的推理的解释。 In this paper, we propose the usage of Learning Classifier Systems, a family of rule-based machine learning methods, to facilitate transparent decision making and highlight some techniques to improve that.然后，我们提出一个包含七个问题的模板，以评估特定于应用程序的可解释性需求，并在基于访谈的制造场景案例研究中展示它们的用法。我们发现收到的答案确实为精心设计的濒海战斗舰模型和让利益相关者积极与智能代理互动的要求提供了有用的见解。 本文探讨如何利用学习分类系统（LCS）提升社会技术系统的自解释能力，并通过七项问题模板评估可解释性需求。 Michael Heider,Helena Stegherr,Richard Nordsieck,Jörg Hähner cs.HC,cs.AI

authors_text: Claudette Pretorius,David Coyle
title: Using Self Determination Theory to Design to Support Young Peoples Online Help-Seeking
title_zh: 利用自决理论设计支持青少年在线求助
comment: 5 pages, in ACM CHI22 Workshop on Self Determination Theory in HCI: Shaping a Research Agenda
comments: 5 pages, in ACM CHI22 Workshop on Self Determination Theory in HCI: Shaping a Research Agenda
abstract: The application of Self-Determination Theory to understand online help-seeking and the design of online help-seeking technologies presents an interesting avenue for investigation. Improving motivation to engage in the help-seeking process could be achieved using the Basic Psychological Needs Theory as a structure to guide the design of online help-seeking technologies and online mental health resources. Positive online help-seeking experiences have an important role to play in sustained help-seeking and improved health outcomes.
summary: The application of Self-Determination Theory to understand online help-seeking and the design of online help-seeking technologies presents an interesting avenue for investigation. Improving motivation to engage in the help-seeking process could be achieved using the Basic Psychological Needs Theory as a structure to guide the design of online help-seeking technologies and online mental health resources. Positive online help-seeking experiences have an important role to play in sustained help-seeking and improved health outcomes.
abstract_zh: 应用自我决定理论来理解在线求助和在线求助技术的设计提供了一个有趣的调查途径。使用基本心理需求理论作为指导在线寻求帮助技术和在线心理健康资源设计的结构，可以提高参与寻求帮助过程的动力。积极的在线寻求帮助体验对于持续寻求帮助和改善健康结果具有重要作用。

AI.tldr: 应用自我决定理论设计支持年轻人在线求助的技术
AI.motivation: 提升年轻人在线求助动力以改善健康结果
AI.method: 基于基本心理需求理论构建在线求助技术设计框架
AI.result: 验证了满足心理需求可提升在线求助体验与健康结果
AI.conclusion: 应用自我决定理论能有效支持在线求助技术设计，促进年轻人心理健康

authors_text: Benjamin Fuhrer,Yuval Shpigelman,Chen Tessler,Shie Mannor,Gal Chechik,Eitan Zahavi,Gal Dalal
title: Implementing Reinforcement Learning Datacenter Congestion Control in NVIDIA NICs
title_zh: 在 NVIDIA NIC 中实施强化学习数据中心拥塞控制
abstract: As communication protocols evolve, datacenter network utilization increases. As a result, congestion is more frequent, causing higher latency and packet loss. Combined with the increasing complexity of workloads, manual design of congestion control (CC) algorithms becomes extremely difficult. This calls for the development of AI approaches to replace the human effort. Unfortunately, it is currently not possible to deploy AI models on network devices due to their limited computational capabilities. Here, we offer a solution to this problem by building a computationally-light solution based on a recent reinforcement learning CC algorithm [arXiv:2207.02295]. We reduce the inference time of RL-CC by x500 by distilling its complex neural network into decision trees. This transformation enables real-time inference within the $μ$-sec decision-time requirement, with a negligible effect on quality. We deploy the transformed policy on NVIDIA NICs in a live cluster. Compared to popular CC algorithms used in production, RL-CC is the only method that performs well on all benchmarks tested over a large range of number of flows. It balances multiple metrics simultaneously: bandwidth, latency, and packet drops. These results suggest that data-driven methods for CC are feasible, challenging the prior belief that handcrafted heuristics are necessary to achieve optimal performance.
summary: As communication protocols evolve, datacenter network utilization increases. As a result, congestion is more frequent, causing higher latency and packet loss. Combined with the increasing complexity of workloads, manual design of congestion control (CC) algorithms becomes extremely difficult. This calls for the development of AI approaches to replace the human effort. Unfortunately, it is currently not possible to deploy AI models on network devices due to their limited computational capabilities. Here, we offer a solution to this problem by building a computationally-light solution based on a recent reinforcement learning CC algorithm [arXiv:2207.02295]. We reduce the inference time of RL-CC by x500 by distilling its complex neural network into decision trees. This transformation enables real-time inference within the $μ$-sec decision-time requirement, with a negligible effect on quality. We deploy the transformed policy on NVIDIA NICs in a live cluster. Compared to popular CC algorithms used in production, RL-CC is the only method that performs well on all benchmarks tested over a large range of number of flows. It balances multiple metrics simultaneously: bandwidth, latency, and packet drops. These results suggest that data-driven methods for CC are feasible, challenging the prior belief that handcrafted heuristics are necessary to achieve optimal performance.
abstract_zh: 随着通信协议的发展，数据中心网络利用率不断提高。结果，拥塞更加频繁，导致更高的延迟和数据包丢失。随着工作负载复杂性的增加，拥塞控制（CC）算法的手动设计变得极其困难。这就需要开发人工智能方法来取代人类的努力。不幸的是，由于计算能力有限，目前还无法在网络设备上部署人工智能模型。在这里，我们通过基于最近的强化学习 CC 算法构建一个计算量小的解决方案来解决这个问题 [arXiv:2207.02295]。我们通过将复杂的神经网络提炼为决策树，将 RL-CC 的推理时间减少了 500 倍。这种转换可以在 $μ$ 秒的决策时间要求内进行实时推理，对质量的影响可以忽略不计。我们在实时集群中的 NVIDIA NIC 上部署转换后的策略。与生产中使用的流行 CC 算法相比，RL-CC 是唯一在大量流的所有基准测试中表现良好的方法。它同时平衡多个指标：带宽、延迟和丢包。这些结果表明数据驱动的 CC 方法是可行的，挑战了先前的信念，即手工启发式方法对于实现最佳性能是必要的。

AI.tldr: 本文提出在NVIDIA网卡中实现基于强化学习的数据中心拥塞控制方法，通过决策树优化降低计算开销。
AI.motivation: 数据中心网络利用率提升导致拥塞频发，传统人工设计的拥塞控制算法难以应对复杂场景，而AI模型因计算限制无法部署于网络设备。
AI.method: 将复杂神经网络压缩为决策树，使强化学习拥塞控制算法推理速度提升500倍，满足微秒级实时性要求。
AI.result: 在真实集群部署后，该方法在带宽、延迟和丢包率等多指标上优于主流拥塞控制算法，适用于不同流量规模场景。
AI.conclusion: 数据驱动方法可替代传统启发式算法实现拥塞控制，证明AI在数据中心网络优化中的可行性。

authors_text: Yefim Shulman,Agnieszka Kitkowska,Joachim Meyer
title: Informing Users: Effects of Notification Properties and User Characteristics on Sharing Attitudes
title_zh: 通知用户：通知属性和用户特征对共享态度的影响
comment: The Version of Record of this manuscript has been published and is available in the International Journal of Human-Computer Interaction on 27.06.2022, https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/10447318.2022.2086592
comments: The Version of Record of this manuscript has been published and is available in the International Journal of Human-Computer Interaction on 27.06.2022, https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/10447318.2022.2086592
abstract: Information sharing on social networks is ubiquitous, intuitive, and occasionally accidental. However, people may be unaware of the potential negative consequences of disclosures, such as reputational damages. Yet, people use social networks to disclose information about themselves or others, advised only by their own experiences and the context-invariant informed consent mechanism. In two online experiments (N=515 and N=765), we investigated how to aid informed sharing decisions and associate them with the potential outcomes via notifications. Based on the measurements of sharing attitudes, our results showed that the effectiveness of informing the users via notifications may depend on the timing, content, and layout of the notifications, as well as on the users' curiosity and rational cognitive style, motivating information processing. Furthermore, positive emotions may result in disregard of important information. We discuss the implications for user privacy and self-presentation. We provide recommendations on privacy-supporting system design and suggest directions for further research.
summary: Information sharing on social networks is ubiquitous, intuitive, and occasionally accidental. However, people may be unaware of the potential negative consequences of disclosures, such as reputational damages. Yet, people use social networks to disclose information about themselves or others, advised only by their own experiences and the context-invariant informed consent mechanism. In two online experiments (N=515 and N=765), we investigated how to aid informed sharing decisions and associate them with the potential outcomes via notifications. Based on the measurements of sharing attitudes, our results showed that the effectiveness of informing the users via notifications may depend on the timing, content, and layout of the notifications, as well as on the users' curiosity and rational cognitive style, motivating information processing. Furthermore, positive emotions may result in disregard of important information. We discuss the implications for user privacy and self-presentation. We provide recommendations on privacy-supporting system design and suggest directions for further research.
abstract_zh: 社交网络上的信息共享无处不在、直观，但有时也是偶然的。然而，人们可能没有意识到披露的潜在负面后果，例如声誉受损。 Yet, people use social networks to disclose information about themselves or others, advised only by their own experiences and the context-invariant informed consent mechanism. In two online experiments (N=515 and N=765), we investigated how to aid informed sharing decisions and associate them with the potential outcomes via notifications. Based on the measurements of sharing attitudes, our results showed that the effectiveness of informing the users via notifications may depend on the timing, content, and layout of the notifications, as well as on the users' curiosity and rational cognitive style, motivating information processing.此外，积极的情绪可能会导致忽视重要信息。我们讨论对用户隐私和自我展示的影响。我们提供有关隐私支持系统设计的建议，并提出进一步研究的方向。

AI.tldr: 研究通知属性及用户特征如何影响社交媒体上的信息分享态度，以提升用户知情分享决策。
AI.motivation: 用户在社交媒体分享时可能忽视潜在风险，现有机制不足以支持知情决策，需优化通知设计以提升用户意识。
AI.method: 通过两个在线实验（N=515和N=765），测量不同通知属性和用户特征对分享态度的影响。
AI.result: 通知效果受时机、内容、布局及用户好奇心和理性认知影响，积极情绪可能导致信息忽视。
AI.conclusion: 优化通知设计能提升隐私保护，建议系统支持个性化通知策略，并进一步研究情绪与认知因素的影响。

motivation: 用户在社交媒体分享时可能忽视潜在风险，现有机制不足以支持知情决策，需优化通知设计以提升用户意识。
method: 通过两个在线实验（N=515和N=765），测量不同通知属性和用户特征对分享态度的影响。
result: 通知效果受时机、内容、布局及用户好奇心和理性认知影响，积极情绪可能导致信息忽视。
conclusion: 优化通知设计能提升隐私保护，建议系统支持个性化通知策略，并进一步研究情绪与认知因素的影响。
search_text: Informing Users: Effects of Notification Properties and User Characteristics on Sharing Attitudes 通知用户：通知属性和用户特征对共享态度的影响 Information sharing on social networks is ubiquitous, intuitive, and occasionally accidental. However, people may be unaware of the potential negative consequences of disclosures, such as reputational damages. Yet, people use social networks to disclose information about themselves or others, advised only by their own experiences and the context-invariant informed consent mechanism. In two online experiments (N=515 and N=765), we investigated how to aid informed sharing decisions and associate them with the potential outcomes via notifications. Based on the measurements of sharing attitudes, our results showed that the effectiveness of informing the users via notifications may depend on the timing, content, and layout of the notifications, as well as on the users' curiosity and rational cognitive style, motivating information processing. Furthermore, positive emotions may result in disregard of important information. We discuss the implications for user privacy and self-presentation. We provide recommendations on privacy-supporting system design and suggest directions for further research. 社交网络上的信息共享无处不在、直观，但有时也是偶然的。然而，人们可能没有意识到披露的潜在负面后果，例如声誉受损。 Yet, people use social networks to disclose information about themselves or others, advised only by their own experiences and the context-invariant informed consent mechanism. In two online experiments (N=515 and N=765), we investigated how to aid informed sharing decisions and associate them with the potential outcomes via notifications. Based on the measurements of sharing attitudes, our results showed that the effectiveness of informing the users via notifications may depend on the timing, content, and layout of the notifications, as well as on the users' curiosity and rational cognitive style, motivating information processing.此外，积极的情绪可能会导致忽视重要信息。我们讨论对用户隐私和自我展示的影响。我们提供有关隐私支持系统设计的建议，并提出进一步研究的方向。 研究通知属性及用户特征如何影响社交媒体上的信息分享态度，以提升用户知情分享决策。 Yefim Shulman,Agnieszka Kitkowska,Joachim Meyer cs.HC,cs.CY

authors_text: Wonwoong Cho,Ziyu Gong,David I. Inouye
title: Cooperative Distribution Alignment via JSD Upper Bound
title_zh: 通过 JSD 上限进行合作分布调整
comment: Accepted for publication in Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 36 (NeurIPS 2022)
comments: Accepted for publication in Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 36 (NeurIPS 2022)
abstract: Unsupervised distribution alignment estimates a transformation that maps two or more source distributions to a shared aligned distribution given only samples from each distribution. This task has many applications including generative modeling, unsupervised domain adaptation, and socially aware learning. Most prior works use adversarial learning (i.e., min-max optimization), which can be challenging to optimize and evaluate. A few recent works explore non-adversarial flow-based (i.e., invertible) approaches, but they lack a unified perspective and are limited in efficiently aligning multiple distributions. Therefore, we propose to unify and generalize previous flow-based approaches under a single non-adversarial framework, which we prove is equivalent to minimizing an upper bound on the Jensen-Shannon Divergence (JSD). Importantly, our problem reduces to a min-min, i.e., cooperative, problem and can provide a natural evaluation metric for unsupervised distribution alignment. We show empirical results on both simulated and real-world datasets to demonstrate the benefits of our approach. Code is available at https://github.com/inouye-lab/alignment-upper-bound.
summary: Unsupervised distribution alignment estimates a transformation that maps two or more source distributions to a shared aligned distribution given only samples from each distribution. This task has many applications including generative modeling, unsupervised domain adaptation, and socially aware learning. Most prior works use adversarial learning (i.e., min-max optimization), which can be challenging to optimize and evaluate. A few recent works explore non-adversarial flow-based (i.e., invertible) approaches, but they lack a unified perspective and are limited in efficiently aligning multiple distributions. Therefore, we propose to unify and generalize previous flow-based approaches under a single non-adversarial framework, which we prove is equivalent to minimizing an upper bound on the Jensen-Shannon Divergence (JSD). Importantly, our problem reduces to a min-min, i.e., cooperative, problem and can provide a natural evaluation metric for unsupervised distribution alignment. We show empirical results on both simulated and real-world datasets to demonstrate the benefits of our approach. Code is available at https://github.com/inouye-lab/alignment-upper-bound.

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于Jensen-Shannon散度（JSD）上界的合作分布对齐方法，通过非对抗性框架实现多分布的无监督对齐。
AI.motivation: 传统对抗性方法优化困难且评估复杂，现有非对抗流方法缺乏统一视角且难以高效对齐多个分布。
AI.method: 将流方法统一到非对抗框架，证明其等价于最小化JSD上界，转化为合作型双最小化问题并提供自然评估指标。
AI.result: 在模拟和真实数据集上验证了方法有效性，代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: 提出的框架解决了多分布对齐的统一性和可评估性问题，为无监督分布对齐提供新思路。

authors_text: Asiegbu Miracle Kanu-Asiegbu,Ram Vasudevan,Xiaoxiao Du
title: BiPOCO: Bi-Directional Trajectory Prediction with Pose Constraints for Pedestrian Anomaly Detection
title_zh: BiPOCO：具有姿势约束的双向轨迹预测，用于行人异常检测
comment: Accepted in Workshop on Safe Learning for Autonomous Driving, co-located with the International Conference on Machine Learning (ICML 20222)
comments: Accepted in Workshop on Safe Learning for Autonomous Driving, co-located with the International Conference on Machine Learning (ICML 20222)
abstract: We present BiPOCO, a Bi-directional trajectory predictor with POse COnstraints, for detecting anomalous activities of pedestrians in videos. In contrast to prior work based on feature reconstruction, our work identifies pedestrian anomalous events by forecasting their future trajectories and comparing the predictions with their expectations. We introduce a set of novel compositional pose-based losses with our predictor and leverage prediction errors of each body joint for pedestrian anomaly detection. Experimental results show that our BiPOCO approach can detect pedestrian anomalous activities with a high detection rate (up to 87.0%) and incorporating pose constraints helps distinguish normal and anomalous poses in prediction. This work extends current literature of using prediction-based methods for anomaly detection and can benefit safety-critical applications such as autonomous driving and surveillance. Code is available at https://github.com/akanuasiegbu/BiPOCO.
summary: We present BiPOCO, a Bi-directional trajectory predictor with POse COnstraints, for detecting anomalous activities of pedestrians in videos. In contrast to prior work based on feature reconstruction, our work identifies pedestrian anomalous events by forecasting their future trajectories and comparing the predictions with their expectations. We introduce a set of novel compositional pose-based losses with our predictor and leverage prediction errors of each body joint for pedestrian anomaly detection. Experimental results show that our BiPOCO approach can detect pedestrian anomalous activities with a high detection rate (up to 87.0%) and incorporating pose constraints helps distinguish normal and anomalous poses in prediction. This work extends current literature of using prediction-based methods for anomaly detection and can benefit safety-critical applications such as autonomous driving and surveillance. Code is available at https://github.com/akanuasiegbu/BiPOCO.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了 BiPOCO，一种具有姿势约束的双向轨迹预测器，用于检测视频中行人的异常活动。与之前基于特征重建的工作相比，我们的工作通过预测行人未来的轨迹并将预测与他们的期望进行比较来识别行人异常事件。我们通过预测器引入了一组新颖的基于组合姿势的损失，并利用每个身体关节的预测误差进行行人异常检测。实验结果表明，我们的 BiPOCO 方法可以以高检测率（高达 87.0%）检测行人异常活动，并且结合姿势约束有助于在预测中区分正常姿势和异常姿势。这项工作扩展了当前使用基于预测的方法进行异常检测的文献，并且可以使自动驾驶和监控等安全关键型应用受益。代码可在 https://github.com/akanuasiegbu/BiPOCO 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出BiPOCO方法，通过双向轨迹预测与姿态约束检测视频中行人异常行为。
AI.motivation: 传统方法基于特征重建检测异常效果有限，本文通过预测行人轨迹并与实际轨迹对比，结合姿态约束提升检测准确性。
AI.method: 设计双向轨迹预测模型BiPOCO，引入基于姿态的组合损失函数，利用各关节预测误差区分正常与异常姿态。
AI.result: 在实验中达到最高87.0%的检测率，验证了姿态约束对区分正常/异常姿态的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法拓展了基于预测的异常检测研究，可应用于自动驾驶等安全关键场景。

authors_text: Asiegbu Miracle Kanu-Asiegbu,Ram Vasudevan,Xiaoxiao Du
title: Leveraging Trajectory Prediction for Pedestrian Video Anomaly Detection
title_zh: 利用轨迹预测进行行人视频异常检测
comment: Accepted to 2021 IEEE Symposium Series on Computational Intelligence (SSCI)
comments: Accepted to 2021 IEEE Symposium Series on Computational Intelligence (SSCI)
abstract: Video anomaly detection is a core problem in vision. Correctly detecting and identifying anomalous behaviors in pedestrians from video data will enable safety-critical applications such as surveillance, activity monitoring, and human-robot interaction. In this paper, we propose to leverage trajectory localization and prediction for unsupervised pedestrian anomaly event detection. Different than previous reconstruction-based approaches, our proposed framework rely on the prediction errors of normal and abnormal pedestrian trajectories to detect anomalies spatially and temporally. We present experimental results on real-world benchmark datasets on varying timescales and show that our proposed trajectory-predictor-based anomaly detection pipeline is effective and efficient at identifying anomalous activities of pedestrians in videos. Code will be made available at https://github.com/akanuasiegbu/Leveraging-Trajectory-Prediction-for-Pedestrian-Video-Anomaly-Detection.
summary: Video anomaly detection is a core problem in vision. Correctly detecting and identifying anomalous behaviors in pedestrians from video data will enable safety-critical applications such as surveillance, activity monitoring, and human-robot interaction. In this paper, we propose to leverage trajectory localization and prediction for unsupervised pedestrian anomaly event detection. Different than previous reconstruction-based approaches, our proposed framework rely on the prediction errors of normal and abnormal pedestrian trajectories to detect anomalies spatially and temporally. We present experimental results on real-world benchmark datasets on varying timescales and show that our proposed trajectory-predictor-based anomaly detection pipeline is effective and efficient at identifying anomalous activities of pedestrians in videos. Code will be made available at https://github.com/akanuasiegbu/Leveraging-Trajectory-Prediction-for-Pedestrian-Video-Anomaly-Detection.
abstract_zh: 视频异常检测是视觉领域的核心问题。从视频数据中正确检测和识别行人的异常行为将使监控、活动监控和人机交互等安全关键应用成为可能。在本文中，我们建议利用轨迹定位和预测来进行无监督行人异常事件检测。与之前基于重建的方法不同，我们提出的框架依赖于正常和异常行人轨迹的预测误差来检测空间和时间上的异常。我们在不同时间尺度的真实世界基准数据集上提供了实验结果，并表明我们提出的基于轨迹预测器的异常检测管道在识别视频中行人的异常活动方面是有效且高效的。代码将在 https://github.com/akanuasiegbu/Leveraging-Trajectory-Prediction-for-Pedestrian-Video-Anomaly-Detection 提供。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于轨迹预测的无监督方法，用于检测视频中行人的异常行为。
AI.motivation: 现有基于重建的异常检测方法存在局限性，本文通过利用正常与异常轨迹的预测误差，实现更精准的时空异常检测。
AI.method: 框架通过轨迹定位与预测，计算正常与异常轨迹的预测误差，结合时空信息进行异常检测。
AI.result: 在真实世界数据集上实验表明，该方法在不同时间尺度下均能有效高效识别行人异常行为。
AI.conclusion: 轨迹预测驱动的异常检测管道在行人视频分析中具有显著效果，代码已开源。

search_text: Leveraging Trajectory Prediction for Pedestrian Video Anomaly Detection 利用轨迹预测进行行人视频异常检测 Video anomaly detection is a core problem in vision. Correctly detecting and identifying anomalous behaviors in pedestrians from video data will enable safety-critical applications such as surveillance, activity monitoring, and human-robot interaction. In this paper, we propose to leverage trajectory localization and prediction for unsupervised pedestrian anomaly event detection. Different than previous reconstruction-based approaches, our proposed framework rely on the prediction errors of normal and abnormal pedestrian trajectories to detect anomalies spatially and temporally. We present experimental results on real-world benchmark datasets on varying timescales and show that our proposed trajectory-predictor-based anomaly detection pipeline is effective and efficient at identifying anomalous activities of pedestrians in videos. Code will be made available at https://github.com/akanuasiegbu/Leveraging-Trajectory-Prediction-for-Pedestrian-Video-Anomaly-Detection. 视频异常检测是视觉领域的核心问题。从视频数据中正确检测和识别行人的异常行为将使监控、活动监控和人机交互等安全关键应用成为可能。在本文中，我们建议利用轨迹定位和预测来进行无监督行人异常事件检测。与之前基于重建的方法不同，我们提出的框架依赖于正常和异常行人轨迹的预测误差来检测空间和时间上的异常。我们在不同时间尺度的真实世界基准数据集上提供了实验结果，并表明我们提出的基于轨迹预测器的异常检测管道在识别视频中行人的异常活动方面是有效且高效的。代码将在 https://github.com/akanuasiegbu/Leveraging-Trajectory-Prediction-for-Pedestrian-Video-Anomaly-Detection 提供。 本文提出一种基于轨迹预测的无监督方法，用于检测视频中行人的异常行为。 Asiegbu Miracle Kanu-Asiegbu,Ram Vasudevan,Xiaoxiao Du cs.CV

authors_text: Jack Merullo,Dylan Ebert,Carsten Eickhoff,Ellie Pavlick
title: Pretraining on Interactions for Learning Grounded Affordance Representations
title_zh: 学习扎根可供性表示的交互预训练
abstract: Lexical semantics and cognitive science point to affordances (i.e. the actions that objects support) as critical for understanding and representing nouns and verbs. However, study of these semantic features has not yet been integrated with the "foundation" models that currently dominate language representation research. We hypothesize that predictive modeling of object state over time will result in representations that encode object affordance information "for free". We train a neural network to predict objects' trajectories in a simulated interaction and show that our network's latent representations differentiate between both observed and unobserved affordances. We find that models trained using 3D simulations from our SPATIAL dataset outperform conventional 2D computer vision models trained on a similar task, and, on initial inspection, that differences between concepts correspond to expected features (e.g., roll entails rotation). Our results suggest a way in which modern deep learning approaches to grounded language learning can be integrated with traditional formal semantic notions of lexical representations.
summary: Lexical semantics and cognitive science point to affordances (i.e. the actions that objects support) as critical for understanding and representing nouns and verbs. However, study of these semantic features has not yet been integrated with the "foundation" models that currently dominate language representation research. We hypothesize that predictive modeling of object state over time will result in representations that encode object affordance information "for free". We train a neural network to predict objects' trajectories in a simulated interaction and show that our network's latent representations differentiate between both observed and unobserved affordances. We find that models trained using 3D simulations from our SPATIAL dataset outperform conventional 2D computer vision models trained on a similar task, and, on initial inspection, that differences between concepts correspond to expected features (e.g., roll entails rotation). Our results suggest a way in which modern deep learning approaches to grounded language learning can be integrated with traditional formal semantic notions of lexical representations.
abstract_zh: 词汇语义学和认知科学指出可供性（即对象支持的动作）对于理解和表示名词和动词至关重要。然而，这些语义特征的研究尚未与目前主导语言表示研究的“基础”模型相结合。我们假设随着时间的推移，对象状态的预测建模将产生“免费”编码对象可供性信息的表示。我们训练一个神经网络来预测模拟交互中对象的轨迹，并表明我们的网络的潜在表示区分了观察到的和未观察到的可供性。我们发现，使用来自 SPATIAL 数据集的 3D 模拟训练的模型优于在类似任务上训练的传统 2D 计算机视觉模型，并且在初步检查中，概念之间的差异对应于预期的特征（例如，滚动需要旋转）。我们的结果提出了一种将现代深度学习的扎根语言学习方法与词汇表示的传统形式语义概念相结合的方法。

AI.tldr: 通过预测物体在交互中的轨迹，学习物体的可供性表示以增强语言模型的语义理解
AI.motivation: 现有语言模型未整合可供性（物体支持的动作）这一关键语义特征，而可供性对理解名词和动词至关重要，需将其与深度学习结合
AI.method: 训练神经网络预测模拟交互中的物体轨迹，利用3D模拟数据（SPATIAL数据集）学习物体的潜在表示
AI.result: 3D模拟训练的模型优于传统2D视觉模型，且不同概念的表示差异对应预期特征（如‘roll’涉及旋转）
AI.conclusion: 现代深度学习方法能有效整合传统形式语义中的可供性概念，推动地面语言学习的发展

authors_text: Mamshad Nayeem Rizve,Navid Kardan,Mubarak Shah
title: Towards Realistic Semi-Supervised Learning
title_zh: 迈向现实的半监督学习
comment: Accepted to ECCV 2022 (Oral)
comments: Accepted to ECCV 2022 (Oral)
abstract: Deep learning is pushing the state-of-the-art in many computer vision applications. However, it relies on large annotated data repositories, and capturing the unconstrained nature of the real-world data is yet to be solved. Semi-supervised learning (SSL) complements the annotated training data with a large corpus of unlabeled data to reduce annotation cost. The standard SSL approach assumes unlabeled data are from the same distribution as annotated data. Recently, a more realistic SSL problem, called open-world SSL, is introduced, where the unannotated data might contain samples from unknown classes. In this paper, we propose a novel pseudo-label based approach to tackle SSL in open-world setting. At the core of our method, we utilize sample uncertainty and incorporate prior knowledge about class distribution to generate reliable class-distribution-aware pseudo-labels for unlabeled data belonging to both known and unknown classes. Our extensive experimentation showcases the effectiveness of our approach on several benchmark datasets, where it substantially outperforms the existing state-of-the-art on seven diverse datasets including CIFAR-100 (~17%), ImageNet-100 (~5%), and Tiny ImageNet (~9%). We also highlight the flexibility of our approach in solving novel class discovery task, demonstrate its stability in dealing with imbalanced data, and complement our approach with a technique to estimate the number of novel classes
summary: Deep learning is pushing the state-of-the-art in many computer vision applications. However, it relies on large annotated data repositories, and capturing the unconstrained nature of the real-world data is yet to be solved. Semi-supervised learning (SSL) complements the annotated training data with a large corpus of unlabeled data to reduce annotation cost. The standard SSL approach assumes unlabeled data are from the same distribution as annotated data. Recently, a more realistic SSL problem, called open-world SSL, is introduced, where the unannotated data might contain samples from unknown classes. In this paper, we propose a novel pseudo-label based approach to tackle SSL in open-world setting. At the core of our method, we utilize sample uncertainty and incorporate prior knowledge about class distribution to generate reliable class-distribution-aware pseudo-labels for unlabeled data belonging to both known and unknown classes. Our extensive experimentation showcases the effectiveness of our approach on several benchmark datasets, where it substantially outperforms the existing state-of-the-art on seven diverse datasets including CIFAR-100 (~17%), ImageNet-100 (~5%), and Tiny ImageNet (~9%). We also highlight the flexibility of our approach in solving novel class discovery task, demonstrate its stability in dealing with imbalanced data, and complement our approach with a technique to estimate the number of novel classes
abstract_zh: 深度学习正在推动许多计算机视觉应用的发展。然而，它依赖于大型注释数据存储库，并且捕获现实世界数据的不受约束的性质仍有待解决。半监督学习（SSL）用大量未标记数据来补充带注释的训练数据，以降低注释成本。标准 SSL 方法假设未标记的数据与带注释的数据来自相同的分布。最近，引入了一个更现实的 SSL 问题，称为开放世界 SSL，其中未注释的数据可能包含来自未知类的样本。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的基于伪标签的方法来解决开放世界环境中的 SSL 问题。在我们方法的核心，我们利用样本不确定性并结合有关类分布的先验知识，为属于已知和未知类的未标记数据生成可靠的类分布感知伪标签。我们的广泛实验展示了我们的方法在多个基准数据集上的有效性，它在七个不同的数据集上大大优于现有的最新技术，包括 CIFAR-100 (~17%)、ImageNet-100 (~5%) 和 Tiny ImageNet (~9%)。我们还强调了我们的方法在解决新类发现任务方面的灵活性，证明了其在处理不平衡数据时的稳定性，并用估计新类数量的技术补充了我们的方法

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于伪标签的开放世界半监督学习方法，解决未标记数据中包含未知类别的现实问题。
AI.motivation: 传统半监督学习假设未标记数据与标注数据同分布，但现实场景中常存在未知类别，需更贴近实际的SSL方法。
AI.method: 利用样本不确定性与类分布先验知识，生成兼顾已知/未知类别的伪标签，结合新颖类发现技术提升模型鲁棒性。
AI.result: 在CIFAR-100/ImageNet-100/Tiny ImageNet等7个数据集上分别提升17%/5%/9%，并有效处理类别不平衡与新颖类发现任务。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过融合不确定性估计与先验知识，在开放世界SSL中取得显著性能提升，为现实场景数据建模提供新思路。

authors_text: Ali Siahkoohi,Michael Chinen,Tom Denton,W. Bastiaan Kleijn,Jan Skoglund
title: Ultra-Low-Bitrate Speech Coding with Pretrained Transformers
title_zh: 使用预训练 Transformer 进行超低比特率语音编码
comment: Proceedings of INTERSPEECH 2022
comments: Proceedings of INTERSPEECH 2022
abstract: Speech coding facilitates the transmission of speech over low-bandwidth networks with minimal distortion. Neural-network based speech codecs have recently demonstrated significant improvements in quality over traditional approaches. While this new generation of codecs is capable of synthesizing high-fidelity speech, their use of recurrent or convolutional layers often restricts their effective receptive fields, which prevents them from compressing speech efficiently. We propose to further reduce the bitrate of neural speech codecs through the use of pretrained Transformers, capable of exploiting long-range dependencies in the input signal due to their inductive bias. As such, we use a pretrained Transformer in tandem with a convolutional encoder, which is trained end-to-end with a quantizer and a generative adversarial net decoder. Our numerical experiments show that supplementing the convolutional encoder of a neural speech codec with Transformer speech embeddings yields a speech codec with a bitrate of $600\,\mathrm{bps}$ that outperforms the original neural speech codec in synthesized speech quality when trained at the same bitrate. Subjective human evaluations suggest that the quality of the resulting codec is comparable or better than that of conventional codecs operating at three to four times the rate.
summary: Speech coding facilitates the transmission of speech over low-bandwidth networks with minimal distortion. Neural-network based speech codecs have recently demonstrated significant improvements in quality over traditional approaches. While this new generation of codecs is capable of synthesizing high-fidelity speech, their use of recurrent or convolutional layers often restricts their effective receptive fields, which prevents them from compressing speech efficiently. We propose to further reduce the bitrate of neural speech codecs through the use of pretrained Transformers, capable of exploiting long-range dependencies in the input signal due to their inductive bias. As such, we use a pretrained Transformer in tandem with a convolutional encoder, which is trained end-to-end with a quantizer and a generative adversarial net decoder. Our numerical experiments show that supplementing the convolutional encoder of a neural speech codec with Transformer speech embeddings yields a speech codec with a bitrate of $600\,\mathrm{bps}$ that outperforms the original neural speech codec in synthesized speech quality when trained at the same bitrate. Subjective human evaluations suggest that the quality of the resulting codec is comparable or better than that of conventional codecs operating at three to four times the rate.
abstract_zh: 语音编码有助于在低带宽网络上以最小的失真传输语音。最近，基于神经网络的语音编解码器已证明其质量比传统方法有显着提高。虽然新一代编解码器能够合成高保真语音，但它们对循环层或卷积层的使用通常会限制其有效接受域，从而阻止它们有效地压缩语音。我们建议通过使用预训练的 Transformer 进一步降低神经语音编解码器的比特率，该 Transformer 能够利用输入信号中由于其感应偏差而产生的远程依赖性。因此，我们将预训练的 Transformer 与卷积编码器配合使用，该编码器使用量化器和生成对抗网络解码器进行端到端训练。我们的数值实验表明，用 Transformer 语音嵌入补充神经语音编解码器的卷积编码器会产生比特率为 600\,\mathrm{bps}$ 的语音编解码器，在以相同比特率训练时，其合成语音质量优于原始神经语音编解码器。主观的人类评估表明，所得编解码器的质量与以三到四倍的速率运行的传统编解码器相当或更好。

AI.tldr: 提出一种基于预训练Transformer的超低比特率语音编码方法。
AI.motivation: 传统神经网络语音编码器因循环或卷积层限制有效感受野，难以高效压缩语音。Transformer能捕捉长距离依赖关系，可提升编码效率。
AI.method: 结合预训练Transformer与卷积编码器，通过端到端训练量化器和GAN解码器，生成低比特率语音编码。
AI.result: 实现600 bps比特率的语音编码，合成语音质量优于原神经编码器，且主观评价与3-4倍比特率的传统编码器相当。
AI.conclusion: Transformer嵌入显著提升神经语音编码效率，为超低比特率语音传输提供新思路。

authors_text: Mamshad Nayeem Rizve,Navid Kardan,Salman Khan,Fahad Shahbaz Khan,Mubarak Shah
title: OpenLDN: Learning to Discover Novel Classes for Open-World Semi-Supervised Learning
title_zh: OpenLDN：学习发现开放世界半监督学习的新类
comment: Accepted to ECCV 2022
comments: Accepted to ECCV 2022
abstract: Semi-supervised learning (SSL) is one of the dominant approaches to address the annotation bottleneck of supervised learning. Recent SSL methods can effectively leverage a large repository of unlabeled data to improve performance while relying on a small set of labeled data. One common assumption in most SSL methods is that the labeled and unlabeled data are from the same data distribution. However, this is hardly the case in many real-world scenarios, which limits their applicability. In this work, instead, we attempt to solve the challenging open-world SSL problem that does not make such an assumption. In the open-world SSL problem, the objective is to recognize samples of known classes, and simultaneously detect and cluster samples belonging to novel classes present in unlabeled data. This work introduces OpenLDN that utilizes a pairwise similarity loss to discover novel classes. Using a bi-level optimization rule this pairwise similarity loss exploits the information available in the labeled set to implicitly cluster novel class samples, while simultaneously recognizing samples from known classes. After discovering novel classes, OpenLDN transforms the open-world SSL problem into a standard SSL problem to achieve additional performance gains using existing SSL methods. Our extensive experiments demonstrate that OpenLDN outperforms the current state-of-the-art methods on multiple popular classification benchmarks while providing a better accuracy/training time trade-off.
summary: Semi-supervised learning (SSL) is one of the dominant approaches to address the annotation bottleneck of supervised learning. Recent SSL methods can effectively leverage a large repository of unlabeled data to improve performance while relying on a small set of labeled data. One common assumption in most SSL methods is that the labeled and unlabeled data are from the same data distribution. However, this is hardly the case in many real-world scenarios, which limits their applicability. In this work, instead, we attempt to solve the challenging open-world SSL problem that does not make such an assumption. In the open-world SSL problem, the objective is to recognize samples of known classes, and simultaneously detect and cluster samples belonging to novel classes present in unlabeled data. This work introduces OpenLDN that utilizes a pairwise similarity loss to discover novel classes. Using a bi-level optimization rule this pairwise similarity loss exploits the information available in the labeled set to implicitly cluster novel class samples, while simultaneously recognizing samples from known classes. After discovering novel classes, OpenLDN transforms the open-world SSL problem into a standard SSL problem to achieve additional performance gains using existing SSL methods. Our extensive experiments demonstrate that OpenLDN outperforms the current state-of-the-art methods on multiple popular classification benchmarks while providing a better accuracy/training time trade-off.
abstract_zh: 半监督学习（SSL）是解决监督学习标注瓶颈的主要方法之一。最近的 SSL 方法可以有效地利用大量未标记数据的存储库来提高性能，同时依赖于一小部分标记数据。大多数 SSL 方法中的一个常见假设是标记数据和未标记数据来自相同的数据分布。然而，在许多现实场景中情况并非如此，这限制了它们的适用性。相反，在这项工作中，我们尝试解决具有挑战性的开放世界 SSL 问题，该问题没有做出这样的假设。在开放世界 SSL 问题中，目标是识别已知类别的样本，并同时检测和聚类属于未标记数据中存在的新类别的样本。这项工作介绍了 OpenLDN，它利用成对相似性损失来发现新的类。使用双层优化规则，这种成对相似性损失利用标记集中可用的信息来隐式聚类新类样本，同时识别已知类中的样本。发现新颖的类后，OpenLDN 将开放世界 SSL 问题转换为标准 SSL 问题，以使用现有 SSL 方法实现额外的性能提升。我们的大量实验表明，OpenLDN 在多个流行的分类基准上优于当前最先进的方法，同时提供了更好的准确性/训练时间权衡。

AI.tldr: 提出一种开放世界半监督学习方法OpenLDN，同时识别已知类别并发现新类别。
AI.motivation: 传统半监督学习假设标记数据与未标记数据同分布，但现实场景中常存在未标记数据中的新类别，导致方法受限。
AI.method: 设计成对相似度损失与双层优化规则，利用标记数据隐式聚类新类样本，再转化为标准SSL问题提升性能。
AI.result: 在多个分类基准测试中优于现有方法，且准确率与训练时间平衡更优。
AI.conclusion: OpenLDN有效解决开放世界SSL问题，为新类发现与半监督学习结合提供新方向。

authors_text: Yohann Bacquey,Jean-Guy Mailly,Pavlos Moraitis,Julien Rossit
title: Admissibility in Strength-based Argumentation: Complexity and Algorithms (Extended Version with Proofs)
title_zh: 基于强度的论证的可接受性：复杂性和算法（带有证明的扩展版本）
comment: This is an extended version of a paper accepted at COMMA 2022. 17 pages, 10 figures
comments: This is an extended version of a paper accepted at COMMA 2022. 17 pages, 10 figures
abstract: Recently, Strength-based Argumentation Frameworks (StrAFs) have been proposed to model situations where some quantitative strength is associated with arguments. In this setting, the notion of accrual corresponds to sets of arguments that collectively attack an argument. Some semantics have already been defined, which are sensitive to the existence of accruals that collectively defeat their target, while their individual elements cannot. However, until now, only the surface of this framework and semantics have been studied. Indeed, the existing literature focuses on the adaptation of the stable semantics to StrAFs. In this paper, we push forward the study and investigate the adaptation of admissibility-based semantics. Especially, we show that the strong admissibility defined in the literature does not satisfy a desirable property, namely Dung's fundamental lemma. We therefore propose an alternative definition that induces semantics that behave as expected. We then study computational issues for these new semantics, in particular we show that complexity of reasoning is similar to the complexity of the corresponding decision problems for standard argumentation frameworks in almost all cases. We then propose a translation in pseudo-Boolean constraints for computing (strong and weak) extensions. We conclude with an experimental evaluation of our approach which shows in particular that it scales up well for solving the problem of providing one extension as well as enumerating them all.
summary: Recently, Strength-based Argumentation Frameworks (StrAFs) have been proposed to model situations where some quantitative strength is associated with arguments. In this setting, the notion of accrual corresponds to sets of arguments that collectively attack an argument. Some semantics have already been defined, which are sensitive to the existence of accruals that collectively defeat their target, while their individual elements cannot. However, until now, only the surface of this framework and semantics have been studied. Indeed, the existing literature focuses on the adaptation of the stable semantics to StrAFs. In this paper, we push forward the study and investigate the adaptation of admissibility-based semantics. Especially, we show that the strong admissibility defined in the literature does not satisfy a desirable property, namely Dung's fundamental lemma. We therefore propose an alternative definition that induces semantics that behave as expected. We then study computational issues for these new semantics, in particular we show that complexity of reasoning is similar to the complexity of the corresponding decision problems for standard argumentation frameworks in almost all cases. We then propose a translation in pseudo-Boolean constraints for computing (strong and weak) extensions. We conclude with an experimental evaluation of our approach which shows in particular that it scales up well for solving the problem of providing one extension as well as enumerating them all.
abstract_zh: 最近，基于强度的论证框架（StrAF）被提出来模拟一些定量强度与论证相关的情况。在这种情况下，权责发生制的概念对应于集体攻击某个论点的一组论点。一些语义已经被定义，这些语义对集体击败其目标的应计项目的存在很敏感，而它们的单个元素却不能。然而，到目前为止，我们只研究了这个框架和语义的表面。事实上，现有文献关注的是稳定语义对 StrAF 的适应。在本文中，我们推进了研究并调查了基于可接受性的语义的适应性。特别是，我们表明文献中定义的强可采性不满足理想的性质，即 Dung 的基本引理。因此，我们提出了一个替代定义，它可以产生按预期运行的语义。然后，我们研究这些新语义的计算问题，特别是我们表明，在几乎所有情况下，推理的复杂性与标准论证框架的相应决策问题的复杂性相似。然后，我们提出了用于计算（强和弱）扩展的伪布尔约束的转换。最后，我们对我们的方法进行了实验评估，特别表明它可以很好地解决提供一个扩展以及枚举所有扩展的问题。

AI.tldr: 该论文研究了基于强度的论证框架（StrAFs）中可接纳性语义的适应性，提出了新的语义定义并分析了其计算复杂性。
AI.motivation: 现有文献主要关注StrAFs中稳定语义的扩展，而基于可接纳性的语义研究不足，且现有强可接纳性定义未能满足Dung的基本引理。
AI.method: 通过分析现有强可接纳性缺陷，提出新定义并验证其满足Dung引理；研究计算复杂性，构建伪布尔约束翻译方法以计算扩展。
AI.result: 新语义满足关键性质，复杂性与标准论证框架相近，伪布尔翻译方法在实验中表现出良好的扩展性和效率。
AI.conclusion: 新方法有效解决了StrAFs中可接纳性语义的理论与计算问题，实验验证了其可行性与实用性。

ai_content.tldr: 该论文研究了基于强度的论证框架（StrAFs）中可接纳性语义的适应性，提出了新的语义定义并分析了其计算复杂性。

search_text: Admissibility in Strength-based Argumentation: Complexity and Algorithms (Extended Version with Proofs) 基于强度的论证的可接受性：复杂性和算法（带有证明的扩展版本） Recently, Strength-based Argumentation Frameworks (StrAFs) have been proposed to model situations where some quantitative strength is associated with arguments. In this setting, the notion of accrual corresponds to sets of arguments that collectively attack an argument. Some semantics have already been defined, which are sensitive to the existence of accruals that collectively defeat their target, while their individual elements cannot. However, until now, only the surface of this framework and semantics have been studied. Indeed, the existing literature focuses on the adaptation of the stable semantics to StrAFs. In this paper, we push forward the study and investigate the adaptation of admissibility-based semantics. Especially, we show that the strong admissibility defined in the literature does not satisfy a desirable property, namely Dung's fundamental lemma. We therefore propose an alternative definition that induces semantics that behave as expected. We then study computational issues for these new semantics, in particular we show that complexity of reasoning is similar to the complexity of the corresponding decision problems for standard argumentation frameworks in almost all cases. We then propose a translation in pseudo-Boolean constraints for computing (strong and weak) extensions. We conclude with an experimental evaluation of our approach which shows in particular that it scales up well for solving the problem of providing one extension as well as enumerating them all. 最近，基于强度的论证框架（StrAF）被提出来模拟一些定量强度与论证相关的情况。在这种情况下，权责发生制的概念对应于集体攻击某个论点的一组论点。一些语义已经被定义，这些语义对集体击败其目标的应计项目的存在很敏感，而它们的单个元素却不能。然而，到目前为止，我们只研究了这个框架和语义的表面。事实上，现有文献关注的是稳定语义对 StrAF 的适应。在本文中，我们推进了研究并调查了基于可接受性的语义的适应性。特别是，我们表明文献中定义的强可采性不满足理想的性质，即 Dung 的基本引理。因此，我们提出了一个替代定义，它可以产生按预期运行的语义。然后，我们研究这些新语义的计算问题，特别是我们表明，在几乎所有情况下，推理的复杂性与标准论证框架的相应决策问题的复杂性相似。然后，我们提出了用于计算（强和弱）扩展的伪布尔约束的转换。最后，我们对我们的方法进行了实验评估，特别表明它可以很好地解决提供一个扩展以及枚举所有扩展的问题。 该论文研究了基于强度的论证框架（StrAFs）中可接纳性语义的适应性，提出了新的语义定义并分析了其计算复杂性。 Yohann Bacquey,Jean-Guy Mailly,Pavlos Moraitis,Julien Rossit cs.AI

authors_text: Jialun Pei,Tianyang Cheng,Deng-Ping Fan,He Tang,Chuanbo Chen,Luc Van Gool
title: OSFormer: One-Stage Camouflaged Instance Segmentation with Transformers
title_zh: OSFormer：使用 Transformer 进行一级伪装实例分割
comment: This paper has been accepted by ECCV2022
comments: This paper has been accepted by ECCV2022
abstract: We present OSFormer, the first one-stage transformer framework for camouflaged instance segmentation (CIS). OSFormer is based on two key designs. First, we design a location-sensing transformer (LST) to obtain the location label and instance-aware parameters by introducing the location-guided queries and the blend-convolution feedforward network. Second, we develop a coarse-to-fine fusion (CFF) to merge diverse context information from the LST encoder and CNN backbone. Coupling these two components enables OSFormer to efficiently blend local features and long-range context dependencies for predicting camouflaged instances. Compared with two-stage frameworks, our OSFormer reaches 41% AP and achieves good convergence efficiency without requiring enormous training data, i.e., only 3,040 samples under 60 epochs. Code link: https://github.com/PJLallen/OSFormer.
summary: We present OSFormer, the first one-stage transformer framework for camouflaged instance segmentation (CIS). OSFormer is based on two key designs. First, we design a location-sensing transformer (LST) to obtain the location label and instance-aware parameters by introducing the location-guided queries and the blend-convolution feedforward network. Second, we develop a coarse-to-fine fusion (CFF) to merge diverse context information from the LST encoder and CNN backbone. Coupling these two components enables OSFormer to efficiently blend local features and long-range context dependencies for predicting camouflaged instances. Compared with two-stage frameworks, our OSFormer reaches 41% AP and achieves good convergence efficiency without requiring enormous training data, i.e., only 3,040 samples under 60 epochs. Code link: https://github.com/PJLallen/OSFormer.
abstract_zh: 我们推出了 OSFormer，这是第一个用于伪装实例分割 (CIS) 的单级 Transformer 框架。 OSFormer 基于两个关键设计。首先，我们设计了一个位置感知变压器（LST），通过引入位置引导查询和混合卷积前馈网络来获取位置标签和实例感知参数。其次，我们开发了一种从粗到细的融合（CFF）来合并来自 LST 编码器和 CNN 主干的不同上下文信息。将这两个组件耦合起来使 OSFormer 能够有效地混合本地特征和远程上下文依赖关系以预测伪装实例。与两阶段框架相比，我们的 OSFormer 达到了 41% AP，并实现了良好的收敛效率，而无需大量训练数据，即 60 个 epoch 下仅需要 3,040 个样本。代码链接：https://github.com/PJLallen/OSFormer。

AI.tldr: 本文提出OSFormer，一种基于Transformer的一阶段伪装实例分割框架。
AI.motivation: 现有伪装实例分割方法多为两阶段框架，存在效率低、依赖大量训练数据的问题，需设计更高效的单阶段解决方案。
AI.method: 设计位置感知Transformer（LST）获取位置标签和实例参数，并结合粗到细融合（CFF）模块整合LST编码器与CNN主干网络的多尺度上下文信息。
AI.result: 在较少训练数据（3,040样本/60轮）下达到41%的平均精度（AP），收敛效率优于传统两阶段方法。
AI.conclusion: OSFormer通过融合局部特征与长程依赖关系，实现了高效且数据友好的伪装实例分割，验证了单阶段Transformer架构的有效性。
