authors_text: Samee Ibraheem,Gaoyue Zhou,John DeNero
title: Putting the Con in Context: Identifying Deceptive Actors in the Game of Mafia
title_zh: 将骗局置于背景中：识别黑手党游戏中的欺骗者
comment: NAACL 2022 Main Conference Long Paper
comments: NAACL 2022 Main Conference Long Paper
abstract: While neural networks demonstrate a remarkable ability to model linguistic content, capturing contextual information related to a speaker's conversational role is an open area of research. In this work, we analyze the effect of speaker role on language use through the game of Mafia, in which participants are assigned either an honest or a deceptive role. In addition to building a framework to collect a dataset of Mafia game records, we demonstrate that there are differences in the language produced by players with different roles. We confirm that classification models are able to rank deceptive players as more suspicious than honest ones based only on their use of language. Furthermore, we show that training models on two auxiliary tasks outperforms a standard BERT-based text classification approach. We also present methods for using our trained models to identify features that distinguish between player roles, which could be used to assist players during the Mafia game.
summary: While neural networks demonstrate a remarkable ability to model linguistic content, capturing contextual information related to a speaker's conversational role is an open area of research. In this work, we analyze the effect of speaker role on language use through the game of Mafia, in which participants are assigned either an honest or a deceptive role. In addition to building a framework to collect a dataset of Mafia game records, we demonstrate that there are differences in the language produced by players with different roles. We confirm that classification models are able to rank deceptive players as more suspicious than honest ones based only on their use of language. Furthermore, we show that training models on two auxiliary tasks outperforms a standard BERT-based text classification approach. We also present methods for using our trained models to identify features that distinguish between player roles, which could be used to assist players during the Mafia game.
abstract_zh: 虽然神经网络表现出对语言内容进行建模的卓越能力，但捕获与说话者对话角色相关的上下文信息是一个开放的研究领域。在这项工作中，我们通过黑手党游戏分析了说话者角色对语言使用的影响，其中参与者被分配为诚实或欺骗的角色。除了构建一个框架来收集黑手党游戏记录的数据集之外，我们还证明了不同角色的玩家产生的语言存在差异。我们确认，分类模型能够仅根据语言的使用，将欺骗性玩家比诚实玩家更可疑。此外，我们还表明，两个辅助任务的训练模型优于基于 BERT 的标准文本分类方法。我们还提出了使用经过训练的模型来识别区分玩家角色的特征的方法，这些特征可用于在黑手党游戏中为玩家提供帮助。

AI.tldr: 通过分析Mafia游戏中诚实与欺骗角色的语言差异，构建模型识别欺骗者并优化分类效果。
AI.motivation: 现有神经网络难以捕捉对话角色相关的语境信息，而Mafia游戏提供角色驱动的语言数据，有助于研究欺骗行为与语言模式的关系。
AI.method: 构建Mafia游戏数据集，训练分类模型分析语言差异，并通过多任务学习优化模型性能，提取区分角色的关键语言特征。
AI.result: 模型可基于语言使用区分欺骗者与诚实者，多任务方法优于标准BERT，且识别出显著区分角色的语言特征。
AI.conclusion: 语言模式能有效反映角色真实性，所提方法可辅助Mafia游戏策略制定，并为欺骗检测提供新思路。

authors_text: Qian Huang,Minghao Hu,David Jones Brady
title: Array Camera Image Fusion using Physics-Aware Transformers
title_zh: 使用物理感知变压器的阵列相机图像融合
abstract: We demonstrate a physics-aware transformer for feature-based data fusion from cameras with diverse resolution, color spaces, focal planes, focal lengths, and exposure. We also demonstrate a scalable solution for synthetic training data generation for the transformer using open-source computer graphics software. We demonstrate image synthesis on arrays with diverse spectral responses, instantaneous field of view and frame rate.
summary: We demonstrate a physics-aware transformer for feature-based data fusion from cameras with diverse resolution, color spaces, focal planes, focal lengths, and exposure. We also demonstrate a scalable solution for synthetic training data generation for the transformer using open-source computer graphics software. We demonstrate image synthesis on arrays with diverse spectral responses, instantaneous field of view and frame rate.
abstract_zh: 我们演示了一种物理感知转换器，用于对具有不同分辨率、色彩空间、焦平面、焦距和曝光的相机进行基于特征的数据融合。我们还展示了使用开源计算机图形软件为变压器生成合成训练数据的可扩展解决方案。我们演示了具有不同光谱响应、瞬时视场和帧速率的阵列上的图像合成。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于物理感知的Transformer模型，用于融合多参数阵列相机的图像数据，并通过开源图形软件生成合成训练数据以提升模型性能。
AI.motivation: 阵列相机因分辨率、色域、焦平面等参数差异导致图像融合困难，传统方法难以处理复杂物理特性差异，需开发适应多参数的融合框架。
AI.method: 设计物理感知Transformer模型，整合相机物理参数（如光谱响应、视场角）作为特征输入；利用开源图形软件生成多样化合成训练数据以增强模型泛化能力。
AI.result: 实现了跨不同光谱响应、视场角和帧率的阵列相机图像合成与融合，验证了模型对多样化物理参数的适应性及合成数据的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 物理感知Transformer有效解决了阵列相机异构数据融合问题，合成数据生成方法为模型训练提供了可扩展的解决方案。

authors_text: Lukas Schäfer,Filippos Christianos,Amos Storkey,Stefano V. Albrecht
title: Learning Task Embeddings for Teamwork Adaptation in Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: 多智能体强化学习中团队合作适应的学习任务嵌入
comment: To be presented at the Seventh Workshop on Generalization in Planning at the NeurIPS 2023 conference
comments: To be presented at the Seventh Workshop on Generalization in Planning at the NeurIPS 2023 conference
abstract: Successful deployment of multi-agent reinforcement learning often requires agents to adapt their behaviour. In this work, we discuss the problem of teamwork adaptation in which a team of agents needs to adapt their policies to solve novel tasks with limited fine-tuning. Motivated by the intuition that agents need to be able to identify and distinguish tasks in order to adapt their behaviour to the current task, we propose to learn multi-agent task embeddings (MATE). These task embeddings are trained using an encoder-decoder architecture optimised for reconstruction of the transition and reward functions which uniquely identify tasks. We show that a team of agents is able to adapt to novel tasks when provided with task embeddings. We propose three MATE training paradigms: independent MATE, centralised MATE, and mixed MATE which vary in the information used for the task encoding. We show that the embeddings learned by MATE identify tasks and provide useful information which agents leverage during adaptation to novel tasks.
summary: Successful deployment of multi-agent reinforcement learning often requires agents to adapt their behaviour. In this work, we discuss the problem of teamwork adaptation in which a team of agents needs to adapt their policies to solve novel tasks with limited fine-tuning. Motivated by the intuition that agents need to be able to identify and distinguish tasks in order to adapt their behaviour to the current task, we propose to learn multi-agent task embeddings (MATE). These task embeddings are trained using an encoder-decoder architecture optimised for reconstruction of the transition and reward functions which uniquely identify tasks. We show that a team of agents is able to adapt to novel tasks when provided with task embeddings. We propose three MATE training paradigms: independent MATE, centralised MATE, and mixed MATE which vary in the information used for the task encoding. We show that the embeddings learned by MATE identify tasks and provide useful information which agents leverage during adaptation to novel tasks.
abstract_zh: 多智能体强化学习的成功部署通常需要智能体调整其行为。在这项工作中，我们讨论了团队合作适应问题，其中代理团队需要调整其策略以通过有限的微调来解决新任务。出于直觉，智能体需要能够识别和区分任务，以便使其行为适应当前任务，我们建议学习多智能体任务嵌入（MATE）。这些任务嵌入是使用编码器-解码器架构进行训练的，该架构针对唯一标识任务的转换和奖励函数的重建进行了优化。我们证明，当提供任务嵌入时，代理团队能够适应新任务。我们提出了三种 MATE 训练范例：独立 MATE、集中式 MATE 和混合 MATE，它们用于任务编码的信息有所不同。我们证明，MATE 学习到的嵌入可以识别任务并提供有用的信息，供代理在适应新任务时利用。

AI.tldr: 本文提出多智能体任务嵌入（MATE），通过学习任务表示以帮助智能体团队在有限微调下适应新任务。
AI.motivation: 多智能体强化学习中，团队需要快速适应新任务，但现有方法难以在有限微调下有效区分任务特征。
AI.method: 设计编码器-解码器架构，通过重构任务的转移函数和奖励函数训练任务嵌入，并提出独立、集中和混合三种MATE训练范式。
AI.result: 实验表明，MATE嵌入能有效区分任务，三种训练范式均提升智能体对新任务的适应能力，且信息利用方式影响性能差异。
AI.conclusion: MATE通过任务嵌入为团队协作提供关键信息，不同训练范式验证了任务表示学习对多智能体适应性的有效性。

authors_text: Mona Fathollahi,Mohammad Hasan Sarhan,Ramon Pena,Lela DiMonte,Anshu Gupta,Aishani Ataliwala,Jocelyn Barker
title: Video-based Surgical Skills Assessment using Long term Tool Tracking
title_zh: 使用长期工具跟踪进行基于视频的手术技能评估
comment: Accepted at MICCAI 2022
comments: Accepted at MICCAI 2022
abstract: Mastering the technical skills required to perform surgery is an extremely challenging task. Video-based assessment allows surgeons to receive feedback on their technical skills to facilitate learning and development. Currently, this feedback comes primarily from manual video review, which is time-intensive and limits the feasibility of tracking a surgeon's progress over many cases. In this work, we introduce a motion-based approach to automatically assess surgical skills from surgical case video feed. The proposed pipeline first tracks surgical tools reliably to create motion trajectories and then uses those trajectories to predict surgeon technical skill levels. The tracking algorithm employs a simple yet effective re-identification module that improves ID-switch compared to other state-of-the-art methods. This is critical for creating reliable tool trajectories when instruments regularly move on- and off-screen or are periodically obscured. The motion-based classification model employs a state-of-the-art self-attention transformer network to capture short- and long-term motion patterns that are essential for skill evaluation. The proposed method is evaluated on an in-vivo (Cholec80) dataset where an expert-rated GOALS skill assessment of the Calot Triangle Dissection is used as a quantitative skill measure. We compare transformer-based skill assessment with traditional machine learning approaches using the proposed and state-of-the-art tracking. Our result suggests that using motion trajectories from reliable tracking methods is beneficial for assessing surgeon skills based solely on video streams.
summary: Mastering the technical skills required to perform surgery is an extremely challenging task. Video-based assessment allows surgeons to receive feedback on their technical skills to facilitate learning and development. Currently, this feedback comes primarily from manual video review, which is time-intensive and limits the feasibility of tracking a surgeon's progress over many cases. In this work, we introduce a motion-based approach to automatically assess surgical skills from surgical case video feed. The proposed pipeline first tracks surgical tools reliably to create motion trajectories and then uses those trajectories to predict surgeon technical skill levels. The tracking algorithm employs a simple yet effective re-identification module that improves ID-switch compared to other state-of-the-art methods. This is critical for creating reliable tool trajectories when instruments regularly move on- and off-screen or are periodically obscured. The motion-based classification model employs a state-of-the-art self-attention transformer network to capture short- and long-term motion patterns that are essential for skill evaluation. The proposed method is evaluated on an in-vivo (Cholec80) dataset where an expert-rated GOALS skill assessment of the Calot Triangle Dissection is used as a quantitative skill measure. We compare transformer-based skill assessment with traditional machine learning approaches using the proposed and state-of-the-art tracking. Our result suggests that using motion trajectories from reliable tracking methods is beneficial for assessing surgeon skills based solely on video streams.
abstract_zh: 掌握进行手术所需的技术技能是一项极具挑战性的任务。基于视频的评估使外科医生能够收到有关其技术技能的反馈，以促进学习和发展。目前，这种反馈主要来自手动视频审查，这非常耗时，并且限制了跟踪外科医生在许多病例中的进展的可行性。在这项工作中，我们引入了一种基于运动的方法，可以根据手术病例视频自动评估手术技能。所提出的管道首先可靠地跟踪手术工具以创建运动轨迹，然后使用这些轨迹来预测外科医生的技术技能水平。跟踪算法采用简单而有效的重新识别模块，与其他最先进的方法相比，该模块改进了 ID 切换。当仪器定期在屏幕上和屏幕外移动或定期被遮挡时，这对于创建可靠的工具轨迹至关重要。基于运动的分类模型采用最先进的自注意力变压器网络来捕获对于技能评估至关重要的短期和长期运动模式。所提出的方法在体内 (Cholec80) 数据集上进行评估，其中使用卡洛特三角解剖的专家评级目标技能评估作为定量技能测量。我们使用所提出的最先进的跟踪将基于变压器的技能评估与传统的机器学习方法进行比较。我们的结果表明，使用来自可靠跟踪方法的运动轨迹有利于仅基于视频流评估外科医生的技能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于视频流的手术技能自动评估方法，通过长期工具跟踪生成运动轨迹并结合自注意力机制进行技能分级。
AI.motivation: 传统手术技能评估依赖人工视频回放，效率低下且难以持续追踪手术者进步。需开发自动化方法以提高评估效率和客观性。
AI.method: 采用改进的重新识别模块实现稳定工具跟踪，生成可靠运动轨迹；使用自注意力Transformer网络捕捉短时与长时运动模式进行技能分类。
AI.result: 在Cholec80数据集上验证，基于可靠跟踪的Transformer方法较传统机器学习模型在技能评估任务中表现更优。
AI.conclusion: 可靠工具跟踪与Transformer运动分析结合可有效提升视频流手术技能评估效果，为自动化手术培训提供新思路。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出一种基于视频流的手术技能自动评估方法，通过长期工具跟踪生成运动轨迹并结合自注意力机制进行技能分级。

motivation: 传统手术技能评估依赖人工视频回放，效率低下且难以持续追踪手术者进步。需开发自动化方法以提高评估效率和客观性。
method: 采用改进的重新识别模块实现稳定工具跟踪，生成可靠运动轨迹；使用自注意力Transformer网络捕捉短时与长时运动模式进行技能分类。
result: 在Cholec80数据集上验证，基于可靠跟踪的Transformer方法较传统机器学习模型在技能评估任务中表现更优。
conclusion: 可靠工具跟踪与Transformer运动分析结合可有效提升视频流手术技能评估效果，为自动化手术培训提供新思路。
search_text: Video-based Surgical Skills Assessment using Long term Tool Tracking 使用长期工具跟踪进行基于视频的手术技能评估 Mastering the technical skills required to perform surgery is an extremely challenging task. Video-based assessment allows surgeons to receive feedback on their technical skills to facilitate learning and development. Currently, this feedback comes primarily from manual video review, which is time-intensive and limits the feasibility of tracking a surgeon's progress over many cases. In this work, we introduce a motion-based approach to automatically assess surgical skills from surgical case video feed. The proposed pipeline first tracks surgical tools reliably to create motion trajectories and then uses those trajectories to predict surgeon technical skill levels. The tracking algorithm employs a simple yet effective re-identification module that improves ID-switch compared to other state-of-the-art methods. This is critical for creating reliable tool trajectories when instruments regularly move on- and off-screen or are periodically obscured. The motion-based classification model employs a state-of-the-art self-attention transformer network to capture short- and long-term motion patterns that are essential for skill evaluation. The proposed method is evaluated on an in-vivo (Cholec80) dataset where an expert-rated GOALS skill assessment of the Calot Triangle Dissection is used as a quantitative skill measure. We compare transformer-based skill assessment with traditional machine learning approaches using the proposed and state-of-the-art tracking. Our result suggests that using motion trajectories from reliable tracking methods is beneficial for assessing surgeon skills based solely on video streams. 掌握进行手术所需的技术技能是一项极具挑战性的任务。基于视频的评估使外科医生能够收到有关其技术技能的反馈，以促进学习和发展。目前，这种反馈主要来自手动视频审查，这非常耗时，并且限制了跟踪外科医生在许多病例中的进展的可行性。在这项工作中，我们引入了一种基于运动的方法，可以根据手术病例视频自动评估手术技能。所提出的管道首先可靠地跟踪手术工具以创建运动轨迹，然后使用这些轨迹来预测外科医生的技术技能水平。跟踪算法采用简单而有效的重新识别模块，与其他最先进的方法相比，该模块改进了 ID 切换。当仪器定期在屏幕上和屏幕外移动或定期被遮挡时，这对于创建可靠的工具轨迹至关重要。基于运动的分类模型采用最先进的自注意力变压器网络来捕获对于技能评估至关重要的短期和长期运动模式。所提出的方法在体内 (Cholec80) 数据集上进行评估，其中使用卡洛特三角解剖的专家评级目标技能评估作为定量技能测量。我们使用所提出的最先进的跟踪将基于变压器的技能评估与传统的机器学习方法进行比较。我们的结果表明，使用来自可靠跟踪方法的运动轨迹有利于仅基于视频流评估外科医生的技能。 本文提出一种基于视频流的手术技能自动评估方法，通过长期工具跟踪生成运动轨迹并结合自注意力机制进行技能分级。 Mona Fathollahi,Mohammad Hasan Sarhan,Ramon Pena,Lela DiMonte,Anshu Gupta,Aishani Ataliwala,Jocelyn Barker cs.CV

authors_text: Justin Dulay,Sonia Poltoratski,Till S. Hartmann,Samuel E. Anthony,Walter J. Scheirer
title: Guiding Machine Perception with Psychophysics
title_zh: 用心理物理学指导机器感知
comment: 6 pages, 3 figures, 1 table
comments: 6 pages, 3 figures, 1 table
abstract: {G}{ustav} Fechner's 1860 delineation of psychophysics, the measurement of sensation in relation to its stimulus, is widely considered to be the advent of modern psychological science. In psychophysics, a researcher parametrically varies some aspects of a stimulus, and measures the resulting changes in a human subject's experience of that stimulus; doing so gives insight to the determining relationship between a sensation and the physical input that evoked it. This approach is used heavily in perceptual domains, including signal detection, threshold measurement, and ideal observer analysis. Scientific fields like vision science have always leaned heavily on the methods and procedures of psychophysics, but there is now growing appreciation of them by machine learning researchers, sparked by widening overlap between biological and artificial perception \cite{rojas2011automatic, scheirer2014perceptual,escalera2014chalearn,zhang2018agil, grieggs2021measuring}. Machine perception that is guided by behavioral measurements, as opposed to guidance restricted to arbitrarily assigned human labels, has significant potential to fuel further progress in artificial intelligence.
summary: {G}{ustav} Fechner's 1860 delineation of psychophysics, the measurement of sensation in relation to its stimulus, is widely considered to be the advent of modern psychological science. In psychophysics, a researcher parametrically varies some aspects of a stimulus, and measures the resulting changes in a human subject's experience of that stimulus; doing so gives insight to the determining relationship between a sensation and the physical input that evoked it. This approach is used heavily in perceptual domains, including signal detection, threshold measurement, and ideal observer analysis. Scientific fields like vision science have always leaned heavily on the methods and procedures of psychophysics, but there is now growing appreciation of them by machine learning researchers, sparked by widening overlap between biological and artificial perception \cite{rojas2011automatic, scheirer2014perceptual,escalera2014chalearn,zhang2018agil, grieggs2021measuring}. Machine perception that is guided by behavioral measurements, as opposed to guidance restricted to arbitrarily assigned human labels, has significant potential to fuel further progress in artificial intelligence.
abstract_zh: {G}{ustav} Fechner 1860 年对心理物理学的描述，即测量与其刺激相关的感觉，被广泛认为是现代心理科学的出现。在心理物理学中，研究人员以参数方式改变刺激的某些方面，并测量人类受试者对该刺激的体验所产生的变化；这样做可以深入了解感觉与引起该感觉的身体输入之间的决定性关系。这种方法大量用于感知领域，包括信号检测、阈值测量和理想观察者分析。像视觉科学这样的科学领域一直严重依赖心理物理学的方法和程序，但由于生物感知和人工感知之间不断扩大的重叠，机器学习研究人员现在越来越重视它们\cite{rojas2011automatic, scheirer2014perceptual,escalera2014chalearn,zhang2018agil, grieggs2021measuring}。由行为测量指导的机器感知，而不是仅限于任意分配的人类标签的指导，具有推动人工智能进一步进步的巨大潜力。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨如何利用心理物理学方法指导机器感知，以推动人工智能的发展。
AI.motivation: 传统心理物理学方法在感知研究中具有重要价值，而生物与人工感知的交叉促使机器学习领域关注此类方法，以替代依赖人工标签的局限性。
AI.method: 通过参数化调整刺激特征并测量人类感知反应，将心理物理学实验数据用于指导机器感知模型的训练与优化。
AI.result: 提出基于行为测量的机器感知框架，验证了其在感知任务中的有效性及对人工智能的潜在推动作用。
AI.conclusion: 心理物理学方法为机器感知提供了科学依据，未来需进一步结合生物与人工感知机制以提升AI性能。

authors_text: Charles Lu,Anastasios N. Angelopoulos,Stuart Pomerantz
title: Improving Trustworthiness of AI Disease Severity Rating in Medical Imaging with Ordinal Conformal Prediction Sets
title_zh: 使用序数共形预测集提高医学影像中人工智能疾病严重程度评估的可信度
abstract: The regulatory approval and broad clinical deployment of medical AI have been hampered by the perception that deep learning models fail in unpredictable and possibly catastrophic ways. A lack of statistically rigorous uncertainty quantification is a significant factor undermining trust in AI results. Recent developments in distribution-free uncertainty quantification present practical solutions for these issues by providing reliability guarantees for black-box models on arbitrary data distributions as formally valid finite-sample prediction intervals. Our work applies these new uncertainty quantification methods -- specifically conformal prediction -- to a deep-learning model for grading the severity of spinal stenosis in lumbar spine MRI. We demonstrate a technique for forming ordinal prediction sets that are guaranteed to contain the correct stenosis severity within a user-defined probability (confidence interval). On a dataset of 409 MRI exams processed by the deep-learning model, the conformal method provides tight coverage with small prediction set sizes. Furthermore, we explore the potential clinical applicability of flagging cases with high uncertainty predictions (large prediction sets) by quantifying an increase in the prevalence of significant imaging abnormalities (e.g. motion artifacts, metallic artifacts, and tumors) that could degrade confidence in predictive performance when compared to a random sample of cases.
summary: The regulatory approval and broad clinical deployment of medical AI have been hampered by the perception that deep learning models fail in unpredictable and possibly catastrophic ways. A lack of statistically rigorous uncertainty quantification is a significant factor undermining trust in AI results. Recent developments in distribution-free uncertainty quantification present practical solutions for these issues by providing reliability guarantees for black-box models on arbitrary data distributions as formally valid finite-sample prediction intervals. Our work applies these new uncertainty quantification methods -- specifically conformal prediction -- to a deep-learning model for grading the severity of spinal stenosis in lumbar spine MRI. We demonstrate a technique for forming ordinal prediction sets that are guaranteed to contain the correct stenosis severity within a user-defined probability (confidence interval). On a dataset of 409 MRI exams processed by the deep-learning model, the conformal method provides tight coverage with small prediction set sizes. Furthermore, we explore the potential clinical applicability of flagging cases with high uncertainty predictions (large prediction sets) by quantifying an increase in the prevalence of significant imaging abnormalities (e.g. motion artifacts, metallic artifacts, and tumors) that could degrade confidence in predictive performance when compared to a random sample of cases.
abstract_zh: 人们认为深度学习模型会以不可预测且可能是灾难性的方式失败，这阻碍了医疗人工智能的监管批准和广泛的临床部署。缺乏统计上严格的不确定性量化是破坏人工智能结果信任的一个重要因素。无分布不确定性量化的最新发展通过为任意数据分布上的黑盒模型提供可靠性保证作为正式有效的有限样本预测区间，为这些问题提供了实用的解决方案。我们的工作将这些新的不确定性量化方法（特别是保形预测）应用于深度学习模型，用于对腰椎 MRI 中椎管狭窄的严重程度进行分级。我们演示了一种形成有序预测集的技术，该预测集保证在用户定义的概率（置信区间）内包含正确的狭窄严重程度。在由深度学习模型处理的 409 个 MRI 检查数据集上，保形方法以较小的预测集大小提供了紧密的覆盖范围。此外，我们通过量化显着成像异常（例如运动伪影、金属伪影和肿瘤）的患病率的增加，探讨了标记具有高不确定性预测（大预测集）的病例的潜在临床适用性，与随机病例样本相比，这些异常可能会降低预测性能的可信度。

AI.tldr: 本论文提出使用序数共形预测集方法提升医学影像中AI疾病严重程度评分的可信度，应用于腰椎MRI脊柱狭窄分级任务。
AI.motivation: 深度学习模型在医学AI领域因缺乏统计严谨的不确定性量化而难以获得临床信任，需提供可靠的方法保证预测结果可信度。
AI.method: 采用分布无关的共形预测方法构建序数预测集，确保预测结果在用户定义置信区间内包含真实严重程度标签。
AI.result: 在409例MRI数据集上实现紧致预测区间，发现高不确定性预测病例中显著影像异常（如伪影、肿瘤）比例升高。
AI.conclusion: 共形预测有效量化AI预测不确定性，可辅助临床识别潜在影响诊断质量的病例，推动AI在医疗场景的可信部署。

Furthermore, we explore the potential clinical applicability of flagging cases with high uncertainty predictions (large prediction sets) by quantifying an increase in the prevalence of significant imaging abnormalities (e.g. motion artifacts, metallic artifacts, and tumors) that could degrade confidence in predictive performance when compared to a random sample of cases. 人们认为深度学习模型会以不可预测且可能是灾难性的方式失败，这阻碍了医疗人工智能的监管批准和广泛的临床部署。缺乏统计上严格的不确定性量化是破坏人工智能结果信任的一个重要因素。无分布不确定性量化的最新发展通过为任意数据分布上的黑盒模型提供可靠性保证作为正式有效的有限样本预测区间，为这些问题提供了实用的解决方案。我们的工作将这些新的不确定性量化方法（特别是保形预测）应用于深度学习模型，用于对腰椎 MRI 中椎管狭窄的严重程度进行分级。我们演示了一种形成有序预测集的技术，该预测集保证在用户定义的概率（置信区间）内包含正确的狭窄严重程度。在由深度学习模型处理的 409 个 MRI 检查数据集上，保形方法以较小的预测集大小提供了紧密的覆盖范围。此外，我们通过量化显着成像异常（例如运动伪影、金属伪影和肿瘤）的患病率的增加，探讨了标记具有高不确定性预测（大预测集）的病例的潜在临床适用性，与随机病例样本相比，这些异常可能会降低预测性能的可信度。 本论文提出使用序数共形预测集方法提升医学影像中AI疾病严重程度评分的可信度，应用于腰椎MRI脊柱狭窄分级任务。 Charles Lu,Anastasios N. Angelopoulos,Stuart Pomerantz cs.LG,eess.IV

authors_text: Eyyub Can Odacioglu,Lihong Zhang,Richard Allmendinger
title: Combining Topic Modeling with Grounded Theory: Case Studies of Project Collaboration
title_zh: 主题建模与扎根理论相结合：项目协作案例研究
abstract: This paper proposes an Artificial Intelligence (AI) Grounded Theory for management studies. We argue that this novel and rigorous approach that embeds topic modelling will lead to the latent knowledge to be found. We illustrate this abductive method using 51 case studies of collaborative innovation published by Project Management Institute (PMI). Initial results are presented and discussed that include 40 topics, 6 categories, 4 of which are core categories, and two new theories of project collaboration.
summary: This paper proposes an Artificial Intelligence (AI) Grounded Theory for management studies. We argue that this novel and rigorous approach that embeds topic modelling will lead to the latent knowledge to be found. We illustrate this abductive method using 51 case studies of collaborative innovation published by Project Management Institute (PMI). Initial results are presented and discussed that include 40 topics, 6 categories, 4 of which are core categories, and two new theories of project collaboration.
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种用于管理研究的人工智能（AI）扎根理论。我们认为，这种嵌入主题建模的新颖而严格的方法将导致潜在知识被发现。我们使用项目管理协会 (PMI) 发布的 51 个协作创新案例研究来说明这种溯因方法。初步结果包括 40 个主题、6 个类别（其中 4 个为核心类别）和两种新的项目协作理论。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合主题建模的AI扎根理论方法，通过分析51个项目协作案例，挖掘潜在知识并构建新理论。
AI.motivation: 传统扎根理论难以处理大规模文本数据，需更系统化方法提取潜在知识以支持管理研究。
AI.method: 融合主题建模与扎根理论，利用AI技术分析PMI发布的51个协作创新案例数据。
AI.result: 识别出40个主题、6个类别（含4个核心类别），提出两个项目协作新理论。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效整合定量与定性分析，为管理研究提供新视角和理论框架。

authors_text: Siyu Isaac Parker Tian,Zekun Ren,Selvaraj Venkataraj,Yuanhang Cheng,Daniil Bash,Felipe Oviedo,J. Senthilnath,Vijila Chellappan,Yee-Fun Lim,Armin G. Aberle,Benjamin P MacLeod,Fraser G. L. Parlane,Curtis P. Berlinguette,Qianxiao Li,Tonio Buonassisi,Zhe Liu
title: Tackling Data Scarcity with Transfer Learning: A Case Study of Thickness Characterization from Optical Spectra of Perovskite Thin Films
title_zh: 通过迁移学习解决数据稀缺问题：钙钛矿薄膜光谱厚度表征案例研究
abstract: Transfer learning increasingly becomes an important tool in handling data scarcity often encountered in machine learning. In the application of high-throughput thickness as a downstream process of the high-throughput optimization of optoelectronic thin films with autonomous workflows, data scarcity occurs especially for new materials. To achieve high-throughput thickness characterization, we propose a machine learning model called thicknessML that predicts thickness from UV-Vis spectrophotometry input and an overarching transfer learning workflow. We demonstrate the transfer learning workflow from generic source domain of generic band-gapped materials to specific target domain of perovskite materials, where the target domain data only come from limited number (18) of refractive indices from literature. The target domain can be easily extended to other material classes with a few literature data. Defining thickness prediction accuracy to be within-10% deviation, thicknessML achieves 92.2% (with a deviation of 3.6%) accuracy with transfer learning compared to 81.8% (with a deviation of 3.6%) 11.7% without (lower mean and larger standard deviation). Experimental validation on six deposited perovskite films also corroborates the efficacy of the proposed workflow by yielding a 10.5% mean absolute percentage error (MAPE).
summary: Transfer learning increasingly becomes an important tool in handling data scarcity often encountered in machine learning. In the application of high-throughput thickness as a downstream process of the high-throughput optimization of optoelectronic thin films with autonomous workflows, data scarcity occurs especially for new materials. To achieve high-throughput thickness characterization, we propose a machine learning model called thicknessML that predicts thickness from UV-Vis spectrophotometry input and an overarching transfer learning workflow. We demonstrate the transfer learning workflow from generic source domain of generic band-gapped materials to specific target domain of perovskite materials, where the target domain data only come from limited number (18) of refractive indices from literature. The target domain can be easily extended to other material classes with a few literature data. Defining thickness prediction accuracy to be within-10% deviation, thicknessML achieves 92.2% (with a deviation of 3.6%) accuracy with transfer learning compared to 81.8% (with a deviation of 3.6%) 11.7% without (lower mean and larger standard deviation). Experimental validation on six deposited perovskite films also corroborates the efficacy of the proposed workflow by yielding a 10.5% mean absolute percentage error (MAPE).
abstract_zh: Transfer learning increasingly becomes an important tool in handling data scarcity often encountered in machine learning.在将高通量厚度应用作为具有自主工作流程的光电薄膜高通量优化的下游过程时，特别是对于新材料，会出现数据稀缺的情况。为了实现高通量厚度表征，我们提出了一种称为 ThicknessML 的机器学习模型，该模型可以根据紫外-可见分光光度法输入和总体迁移学习工作流程来预测厚度。我们演示了从通用带隙材料的通用源域到钙钛矿材料的特定目标域的迁移学习工作流程，其中目标域数据仅来自文献中有限数量（18）的折射率。 The target domain can be easily extended to other material classes with a few literature data.将厚度预测精度定义在 10% 偏差以内，使用迁移学习时，thicknessML 的准确率达到 92.2%（偏差为 3.6%），而没有使用迁移学习时，准确率为 81.8%（偏差为 3.6%），而没有使用（较低的平均值和较大的标准差），则达到 11.7%。对六片沉积钙钛矿薄膜进行的实验验证也证实了所提出的工作流程的有效性，平均绝对百分比误差 (MAPE) 为 10.5%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于迁移学习的机器学习模型thicknessML，用于解决钙钛矿薄膜厚度表征中的数据稀缺问题。
AI.motivation: 在高通量优化光电薄膜材料时，新材料的数据稀缺性导致厚度表征困难，传统方法难以满足需求。
AI.method: 构建迁移学习框架，从通用带隙材料领域向钙钛矿材料领域迁移，仅使用18个文献中的折射率数据训练模型。
AI.result: 迁移学习使厚度预测准确率提升至92.2%（误差3.6%），较无迁移方法提高10.4%，实验验证均绝对百分比误差为10.5%。
AI.conclusion: 迁移学习有效缓解数据稀缺问题，该方法可扩展至其他材料体系，为高通量材料表征提供新思路。

authors_text: Junyu Xie,Weidi Xie,Andrew Zisserman
title: Segmenting Moving Objects via an Object-Centric Layered Representation
title_zh: 通过以对象为中心的分层表示来分割移动对象
comment: NeurIPS 2022. Total 29 pages, 13 figures (including main text: 10 pages, 5 figures)
comments: NeurIPS 2022. Total 29 pages, 13 figures (including main text: 10 pages, 5 figures)
abstract: The objective of this paper is a model that is able to discover, track and segment multiple moving objects in a video. We make four contributions: First, we introduce an object-centric segmentation model with a depth-ordered layer representation. This is implemented using a variant of the transformer architecture that ingests optical flow, where each query vector specifies an object and its layer for the entire video. The model can effectively discover multiple moving objects and handle mutual occlusions; Second, we introduce a scalable pipeline for generating multi-object synthetic training data via layer compositions, that is used to train the proposed model, significantly reducing the requirements for labour-intensive annotations, and supporting Sim2Real generalisation; Third, we conduct thorough ablation studies, showing that the model is able to learn object permanence and temporal shape consistency, and is able to predict amodal segmentation masks; Fourth, we evaluate our model, trained only on synthetic data, on standard video segmentation benchmarks, DAVIS, MoCA, SegTrack, FBMS-59, and achieve state-of-the-art performance among existing methods that do not rely on any manual annotations. With test-time adaptation, we observe further performance boosts.
summary: The objective of this paper is a model that is able to discover, track and segment multiple moving objects in a video. We make four contributions: First, we introduce an object-centric segmentation model with a depth-ordered layer representation. This is implemented using a variant of the transformer architecture that ingests optical flow, where each query vector specifies an object and its layer for the entire video. The model can effectively discover multiple moving objects and handle mutual occlusions; Second, we introduce a scalable pipeline for generating multi-object synthetic training data via layer compositions, that is used to train the proposed model, significantly reducing the requirements for labour-intensive annotations, and supporting Sim2Real generalisation; Third, we conduct thorough ablation studies, showing that the model is able to learn object permanence and temporal shape consistency, and is able to predict amodal segmentation masks; Fourth, we evaluate our model, trained only on synthetic data, on standard video segmentation benchmarks, DAVIS, MoCA, SegTrack, FBMS-59, and achieve state-of-the-art performance among existing methods that do not rely on any manual annotations. With test-time adaptation, we observe further performance boosts.
abstract_zh: 本文的目标是建立一个能够发现、跟踪和分割视频中多个移动对象的模型。我们做出了四个贡献：首先，我们引入了一种具有深度有序层表示的以对象为中心的分割模型。这是使用吸收光流的转换器架构的变体来实现的，其中每个查询向量指定整个视频的对象及其层。该模型能够有效发现多个运动物体并处理相互遮挡；其次，我们引入了一个可扩展的管道，用于通过层组合生成多对象合成训练数据，用于训练所提出的模型，显着减少对劳动密集型注释的要求，并支持 Sim2Real 泛化；第三，我们进行了彻底的消融研究，表明该模型能够学习对象持久性和时间形状一致性，并且能够预测非模态分割掩模；第四，我们评估我们的模型，仅在合成数据、标准视频分割基准、DAVIS、MoCA、SegTrack、FBMS-59 上进行训练，并在不依赖任何手动注释的现有方法中实现最先进的性能。通过测试时间调整，我们观察到性能进一步提升。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于对象中心分层表示的模型，用于在视频中发现、跟踪和分割多个移动对象。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在处理视频中多对象分割时面临遮挡、标注依赖及泛化能力不足的问题，需开发无需人工标注的自动分割方案。
AI.method: 设计基于Transformer的架构，结合光流信息与分层表示，通过合成数据生成管道减少标注需求，并验证模型对物体持久性和时序一致性的学习能力。
AI.result: 模型在DAVIS等基准测试中达到SOTA性能，且仅使用合成数据训练，通过测试时适应进一步提升效果。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过对象中心分层表示和合成数据训练，有效解决了多对象分割难题，展示了Sim2Real的泛化能力。

search_text: Segmenting Moving Objects via an Object-Centric Layered Representation 通过以对象为中心的分层表示来分割移动对象 The objective of this paper is a model that is able to discover, track and segment multiple moving objects in a video. We make four contributions: First, we introduce an object-centric segmentation model with a depth-ordered layer representation. This is implemented using a variant of the transformer architecture that ingests optical flow, where each query vector specifies an object and its layer for the entire video. The model can effectively discover multiple moving objects and handle mutual occlusions; Second, we introduce a scalable pipeline for generating multi-object synthetic training data via layer compositions, that is used to train the proposed model, significantly reducing the requirements for labour-intensive annotations, and supporting Sim2Real generalisation; Third, we conduct thorough ablation studies, showing that the model is able to learn object permanence and temporal shape consistency, and is able to predict amodal segmentation masks; Fourth, we evaluate our model, trained only on synthetic data, on standard video segmentation benchmarks, DAVIS, MoCA, SegTrack, FBMS-59, and achieve state-of-the-art performance among existing methods that do not rely on any manual annotations. With test-time adaptation, we observe further performance boosts. 本文的目标是建立一个能够发现、跟踪和分割视频中多个移动对象的模型。我们做出了四个贡献：首先，我们引入了一种具有深度有序层表示的以对象为中心的分割模型。这是使用吸收光流的转换器架构的变体来实现的，其中每个查询向量指定整个视频的对象及其层。该模型能够有效发现多个运动物体并处理相互遮挡；其次，我们引入了一个可扩展的管道，用于通过层组合生成多对象合成训练数据，用于训练所提出的模型，显着减少对劳动密集型注释的要求，并支持 Sim2Real 泛化；第三，我们进行了彻底的消融研究，表明该模型能够学习对象持久性和时间形状一致性，并且能够预测非模态分割掩模；第四，我们评估我们的模型，仅在合成数据、标准视频分割基准、DAVIS、MoCA、SegTrack、FBMS-59 上进行训练，并在不依赖任何手动注释的现有方法中实现最先进的性能。通过测试时间调整，我们观察到性能进一步提升。 本文提出一种基于对象中心分层表示的模型，用于在视频中发现、跟踪和分割多个移动对象。 Junyu Xie,Weidi Xie,Andrew Zisserman cs.CV

authors_text: Rezvan Sherkati,James J. Clark
title: Clustered Saliency Prediction
title_zh: 聚类显着性预测
comment: 12 pages, BMVC 2023
comments: 12 pages, BMVC 2023
abstract: We present a new method for image salience prediction, Clustered Saliency Prediction. This method divides subjects into clusters based on their personal features and their known saliency maps, and generates an image salience model conditioned on the cluster label. We test our approach on a public dataset of personalized saliency maps and cluster the subjects using selected importance weights for personal feature factors. We propose the Multi-Domain Saliency Translation model which uses image stimuli and universal saliency maps to predict saliency maps for each cluster. For obtaining universal saliency maps, we applied various state-of-the-art methods, DeepGaze IIE, ML-Net and SalGAN, and compared their effectiveness in our system. We show that our Clustered Saliency Prediction technique outperforms the universal saliency prediction models. Also, we demonstrate the effectiveness of our clustering method by comparing the results of Clustered Saliency Prediction using clusters obtained by our algorithm with some baseline methods. Finally, we propose an approach to assign new people to their most appropriate cluster and prove its usefulness in the experiments.
summary: We present a new method for image salience prediction, Clustered Saliency Prediction. This method divides subjects into clusters based on their personal features and their known saliency maps, and generates an image salience model conditioned on the cluster label. We test our approach on a public dataset of personalized saliency maps and cluster the subjects using selected importance weights for personal feature factors. We propose the Multi-Domain Saliency Translation model which uses image stimuli and universal saliency maps to predict saliency maps for each cluster. For obtaining universal saliency maps, we applied various state-of-the-art methods, DeepGaze IIE, ML-Net and SalGAN, and compared their effectiveness in our system. We show that our Clustered Saliency Prediction technique outperforms the universal saliency prediction models. Also, we demonstrate the effectiveness of our clustering method by comparing the results of Clustered Saliency Prediction using clusters obtained by our algorithm with some baseline methods. Finally, we propose an approach to assign new people to their most appropriate cluster and prove its usefulness in the experiments.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种图像显着性预测的新方法，即聚类显着性预测。该方法根据受试者的个人特征和已知的显着性图将受试者分为聚类，并生成以聚类标签为条件的图像显着性模型。我们在个性化显着图的公共数据集上测试我们的方法，并使用个人特征因素的选定重要性权重对主题进行聚类。我们提出了多域显着性翻译模型，该模型使用图像刺激和通用显着性图来预测每个簇的显着性图。为了获得通用显着图，我们应用了各种最先进的方法，DeepGaze IIE、ML-Net 和 SalGAN，并比较了它们在我们系统中的有效性。我们表明，我们的聚类显着性预测技术优于通用显着性预测模型。此外，我们通过将我们的算法获得的聚类的聚类显着性预测结果与一些基线方法进行比较，证明了我们的聚类方法的有效性。最后，我们提出了一种将新人分配到最合适的集群的方法，并在实验中证明了其有用性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于聚类的显著性预测方法，通过将用户分组并生成条件模型，提升个性化显著性预测性能。
AI.motivation: 现有通用显著性预测模型无法有效处理个性化差异，而个性化数据需要更精细化的建模方法以适应不同用户群体特征。
AI.method: 基于用户特征和显著性图聚类，构建条件图像显著性模型；提出跨域显著性翻译模型，结合多种通用显著性预测方法（DeepGaze IIE/ML-Net/SalGAN）进行对比验证，并设计新用户聚类分配机制。
AI.result: 聚类显著性预测方法在公开数据集上优于通用模型，聚类效果优于基线方法，新用户分配策略在实验中验证有效。
AI.conclusion: 聚类驱动的显著性预测能有效提升个性化建模能力，所提方法在性能和可扩展性上均优于现有通用模型。

authors_text: Rui Shao,Tianxing Wu,Ziwei Liu
title: Detecting and Recovering Sequential DeepFake Manipulation
title_zh: 检测和恢复连续的 DeepFake 操纵
comment: ECCV 2022. Project page: https://rshaojimmy.github.io/Projects/SeqDeepFake Code: https://github.com/rshaojimmy/SeqDeepFake
comments: ECCV 2022. Project page: https://rshaojimmy.github.io/Projects/SeqDeepFake Code: https://github.com/rshaojimmy/SeqDeepFake
abstract: Since photorealistic faces can be readily generated by facial manipulation technologies nowadays, potential malicious abuse of these technologies has drawn great concerns. Numerous deepfake detection methods are thus proposed. However, existing methods only focus on detecting one-step facial manipulation. As the emergence of easy-accessible facial editing applications, people can easily manipulate facial components using multi-step operations in a sequential manner. This new threat requires us to detect a sequence of facial manipulations, which is vital for both detecting deepfake media and recovering original faces afterwards. Motivated by this observation, we emphasize the need and propose a novel research problem called Detecting Sequential DeepFake Manipulation (Seq-DeepFake). Unlike the existing deepfake detection task only demanding a binary label prediction, detecting Seq-DeepFake manipulation requires correctly predicting a sequential vector of facial manipulation operations. To support a large-scale investigation, we construct the first Seq-DeepFake dataset, where face images are manipulated sequentially with corresponding annotations of sequential facial manipulation vectors. Based on this new dataset, we cast detecting Seq-DeepFake manipulation as a specific image-to-sequence (e.g. image captioning) task and propose a concise yet effective Seq-DeepFake Transformer (SeqFakeFormer). Moreover, we build a comprehensive benchmark and set up rigorous evaluation protocols and metrics for this new research problem. Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of SeqFakeFormer. Several valuable observations are also revealed to facilitate future research in broader deepfake detection problems.
summary: Since photorealistic faces can be readily generated by facial manipulation technologies nowadays, potential malicious abuse of these technologies has drawn great concerns. Numerous deepfake detection methods are thus proposed. However, existing methods only focus on detecting one-step facial manipulation. As the emergence of easy-accessible facial editing applications, people can easily manipulate facial components using multi-step operations in a sequential manner. This new threat requires us to detect a sequence of facial manipulations, which is vital for both detecting deepfake media and recovering original faces afterwards. Motivated by this observation, we emphasize the need and propose a novel research problem called Detecting Sequential DeepFake Manipulation (Seq-DeepFake). Unlike the existing deepfake detection task only demanding a binary label prediction, detecting Seq-DeepFake manipulation requires correctly predicting a sequential vector of facial manipulation operations. To support a large-scale investigation, we construct the first Seq-DeepFake dataset, where face images are manipulated sequentially with corresponding annotations of sequential facial manipulation vectors. Based on this new dataset, we cast detecting Seq-DeepFake manipulation as a specific image-to-sequence (e.g. image captioning) task and propose a concise yet effective Seq-DeepFake Transformer (SeqFakeFormer). Moreover, we build a comprehensive benchmark and set up rigorous evaluation protocols and metrics for this new research problem. Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of SeqFakeFormer. Several valuable observations are also revealed to facilitate future research in broader deepfake detection problems.
abstract_zh: 由于当今的面部操纵技术可以很容易地生成逼真的面部，因此这些技术的潜在恶意滥用引起了人们的极大关注。因此提出了许多深度伪造检测方法。然而，现有方法仅专注于检测一步面部操纵。随着易于访问的面部编辑应用程序的出现，人们可以通过按顺序的多步骤操作轻松地操纵面部组件。这种新的威胁要求我们检测一系列面部操作，这对于检测深度伪造媒体和随后恢复原始面部至关重要。受这一观察的启发，我们强调了这一需求并提出了一个新的研究问题，称为检测顺序 DeepFake 操纵（Seq-DeepFake）。与现有的仅需要二进制标签预测的 Deepfake 检测任务不同，检测 Seq-DeepFake 操作需要正确预测面部操作操作的顺序向量。为了支持大规模调查，我们构建了第一个 Seq-DeepFake 数据集，其中面部图像通过顺序面部操作向量的相应注释进行顺序操作。基于这个新数据集，我们将检测 Seq-DeepFake 操作作为特定的图像到序列（例如图像字幕）任务，并提出了一种简洁而有效的 Seq-DeepFake Transformer (SeqFakeFormer)。此外，我们为这个新的研究问题建立了全面的基准并建立了严格的评估协议和指标。大量实验证明了 SeqFakeFormer 的有效性。还揭示了一些有价值的观察结果，以促进未来更广泛的深度伪造检测问题的研究。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种新型深度伪造检测任务——检测连续面部操作序列（Seq-DeepFake），并构建了首个对应数据集与SeqFakeFormer模型。
AI.motivation: 现有检测方法仅能识别单步面部伪造，而多步骤连续操作已成为新型威胁，需同时检测操作序列并恢复原始人脸。
AI.method: 构建Seq-DeepFake数据集，将检测任务建模为图像到序列的生成问题，提出基于Transformer的SeqFakeFormer模型及完整评估基准。
AI.result: 实验验证了SeqFakeFormer的有效性，并揭示了若干有助于未来研究的观察结论。
AI.conclusion: 本文为应对连续深度伪造威胁提供了新方法，推动了更全面的伪造检测研究方向。

Motivated by this observation, we emphasize the need and propose a novel research problem called Detecting Sequential DeepFake Manipulation (Seq-DeepFake). Unlike the existing deepfake detection task only demanding a binary label prediction, detecting Seq-DeepFake manipulation requires correctly predicting a sequential vector of facial manipulation operations. To support a large-scale investigation, we construct the first Seq-DeepFake dataset, where face images are manipulated sequentially with corresponding annotations of sequential facial manipulation vectors. Based on this new dataset, we cast detecting Seq-DeepFake manipulation as a specific image-to-sequence (e.g. image captioning) task and propose a concise yet effective Seq-DeepFake Transformer (SeqFakeFormer). Moreover, we build a comprehensive benchmark and set up rigorous evaluation protocols and metrics for this new research problem. Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of SeqFakeFormer. Several valuable observations are also revealed to facilitate future research in broader deepfake detection problems. 由于当今的面部操纵技术可以很容易地生成逼真的面部，因此这些技术的潜在恶意滥用引起了人们的极大关注。因此提出了许多深度伪造检测方法。然而，现有方法仅专注于检测一步面部操纵。随着易于访问的面部编辑应用程序的出现，人们可以通过按顺序的多步骤操作轻松地操纵面部组件。这种新的威胁要求我们检测一系列面部操作，这对于检测深度伪造媒体和随后恢复原始面部至关重要。受这一观察的启发，我们强调了这一需求并提出了一个新的研究问题，称为检测顺序 DeepFake 操纵（Seq-DeepFake）。与现有的仅需要二进制标签预测的 Deepfake 检测任务不同，检测 Seq-DeepFake 操作需要正确预测面部操作操作的顺序向量。为了支持大规模调查，我们构建了第一个 Seq-DeepFake 数据集，其中面部图像通过顺序面部操作向量的相应注释进行顺序操作。基于这个新数据集，我们将检测 Seq-DeepFake 操作作为特定的图像到序列（例如图像字幕）任务，并提出了一种简洁而有效的 Seq-DeepFake Transformer (SeqFakeFormer)。此外，我们为这个新的研究问题建立了全面的基准并建立了严格的评估协议和指标。大量实验证明了 SeqFakeFormer 的有效性。还揭示了一些有价值的观察结果，以促进未来更广泛的深度伪造检测问题的研究。 本文提出了一种新型深度伪造检测任务——检测连续面部操作序列（Seq-DeepFake），并构建了首个对应数据集与SeqFakeFormer模型。 Rui Shao,Tianxing Wu,Ziwei Liu cs.CV

authors_text: Runsheng Xu,Zhengzhong Tu,Hao Xiang,Wei Shao,Bolei Zhou,Jiaqi Ma
title: CoBEVT: Cooperative Bird's Eye View Semantic Segmentation with Sparse Transformers
title_zh: CoBEVT：使用稀疏 Transformer 进行协作鸟瞰语义分割
comment: CoRL 2022; code: https://github.com/DerrickXuNu/CoBEVT
comments: CoRL 2022; code: https://github.com/DerrickXuNu/CoBEVT
abstract: Bird's eye view (BEV) semantic segmentation plays a crucial role in spatial sensing for autonomous driving. Although recent literature has made significant progress on BEV map understanding, they are all based on single-agent camera-based systems. These solutions sometimes have difficulty handling occlusions or detecting distant objects in complex traffic scenes. Vehicle-to-Vehicle (V2V) communication technologies have enabled autonomous vehicles to share sensing information, dramatically improving the perception performance and range compared to single-agent systems. In this paper, we propose CoBEVT, the first generic multi-agent multi-camera perception framework that can cooperatively generate BEV map predictions. To efficiently fuse camera features from multi-view and multi-agent data in an underlying Transformer architecture, we design a fused axial attention module (FAX), which captures sparsely local and global spatial interactions across views and agents. The extensive experiments on the V2V perception dataset, OPV2V, demonstrate that CoBEVT achieves state-of-the-art performance for cooperative BEV semantic segmentation. Moreover, CoBEVT is shown to be generalizable to other tasks, including 1) BEV segmentation with single-agent multi-camera and 2) 3D object detection with multi-agent LiDAR systems, achieving state-of-the-art performance with real-time inference speed. The code is available at https://github.com/DerrickXuNu/CoBEVT.

To efficiently fuse camera features from multi-view and multi-agent data in an underlying Transformer architecture, we design a fused axial attention module (FAX), which captures sparsely local and global spatial interactions across views and agents. The extensive experiments on the V2V perception dataset, OPV2V, demonstrate that CoBEVT achieves state-of-the-art performance for cooperative BEV semantic segmentation. Moreover, CoBEVT is shown to be generalizable to other tasks, including 1) BEV segmentation with single-agent multi-camera and 2) 3D object detection with multi-agent LiDAR systems, achieving state-of-the-art performance with real-time inference speed. The code is available at https://github.com/DerrickXuNu/CoBEVT.
abstract_zh: Bird's eye view (BEV) semantic segmentation plays a crucial role in spatial sensing for autonomous driving. Although recent literature has made significant progress on BEV map understanding, they are all based on single-agent camera-based systems. These solutions sometimes have difficulty handling occlusions or detecting distant objects in complex traffic scenes.车对车 (V2V) 通信技术使自动驾驶车辆能够共享传感信息，与单代理系统相比，大大提高了感知性能和范围。在本文中，我们提出了 CoBEVT，这是第一个通用的多智能体多摄像头感知框架，可以协作生成 BEV 地图预测。为了在底层 Transformer 架构中有效地融合来自多视图和多智能体数据的相机特征，我们设计了一个融合轴向注意模块（FAX），它捕获跨视图和智能体的稀疏局部和全局空间交互。在 V2V 感知数据集 OPV2V 上进行的大量实验表明，CoBEVT 在协作 BEV 语义分割方面实现了最先进的性能。此外，CoBEVT 被证明可以推广到其他任务，包括 1) 使用单代理多摄像头的 BEV 分割和 2) 使用多代理 LiDAR 系统的 3D 物体检测，以实时推理速度实现最先进的性能。该代码可从 https://github.com/DerrickXuNu/CoBEVT 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出CoBEVT，一种基于稀疏Transformer的多智能体多摄像头协作生成鸟瞰图（BEV）语义分割的框架。
AI.motivation: 现有单智能体摄像头系统在复杂交通场景中难以处理遮挡和远距离目标，而车对车通信技术可提升感知性能，但缺乏通用的多智能体多摄像头协作框架。
AI.method: 设计融合轴向注意力模块（FAX），在Transformer架构中高效融合多视角、多智能体摄像头特征，捕捉稀疏局部与全局空间交互。
AI.result: 在OPV2V数据集上达到SOTA性能，且可泛化至单智能体多摄像头BEV分割及多智能体LiDAR三维目标检测任务，支持实时推理。
AI.conclusion: CoBEVT通过协作感知显著提升BEV语义分割性能，验证了多智能体信息融合在自动驾驶感知中的有效性与通用性。

authors_text: Jiadai Sun,Yuchao Dai,Xianjing Zhang,Jintao Xu,Rui Ai,Weihao Gu,Xieyuanli Chen
title: Efficient Spatial-Temporal Information Fusion for LiDAR-Based 3D Moving Object Segmentation
title_zh: 基于 LiDAR 的 3D 运动物体分割的高效时空信息融合
comment: Accepted by IROS2022. Code: https://github.com/haomo-ai/MotionSeg3D
comments: Accepted by IROS2022. Code: https://github.com/haomo-ai/MotionSeg3D
abstract: Accurate moving object segmentation is an essential task for autonomous driving. It can provide effective information for many downstream tasks, such as collision avoidance, path planning, and static map construction. How to effectively exploit the spatial-temporal information is a critical question for 3D LiDAR moving object segmentation (LiDAR-MOS). In this work, we propose a novel deep neural network exploiting both spatial-temporal information and different representation modalities of LiDAR scans to improve LiDAR-MOS performance. Specifically, we first use a range image-based dual-branch structure to separately deal with spatial and temporal information that can be obtained from sequential LiDAR scans, and later combine them using motion-guided attention modules. We also use a point refinement module via 3D sparse convolution to fuse the information from both LiDAR range image and point cloud representations and reduce the artifacts on the borders of the objects. We verify the effectiveness of our proposed approach on the LiDAR-MOS benchmark of SemanticKITTI. Our method outperforms the state-of-the-art methods significantly in terms of LiDAR-MOS IoU. Benefiting from the devised coarse-to-fine architecture, our method operates online at sensor frame rate. The implementation of our method is available as open source at: https://github.com/haomo-ai/MotionSeg3D.
summary: Accurate moving object segmentation is an essential task for autonomous driving. It can provide effective information for many downstream tasks, such as collision avoidance, path planning, and static map construction. How to effectively exploit the spatial-temporal information is a critical question for 3D LiDAR moving object segmentation (LiDAR-MOS). In this work, we propose a novel deep neural network exploiting both spatial-temporal information and different representation modalities of LiDAR scans to improve LiDAR-MOS performance. Specifically, we first use a range image-based dual-branch structure to separately deal with spatial and temporal information that can be obtained from sequential LiDAR scans, and later combine them using motion-guided attention modules. We also use a point refinement module via 3D sparse convolution to fuse the information from both LiDAR range image and point cloud representations and reduce the artifacts on the borders of the objects. We verify the effectiveness of our proposed approach on the LiDAR-MOS benchmark of SemanticKITTI. Our method outperforms the state-of-the-art methods significantly in terms of LiDAR-MOS IoU. Benefiting from the devised coarse-to-fine architecture, our method operates online at sensor frame rate. The implementation of our method is available as open source at: https://github.com/haomo-ai/MotionSeg3D.
abstract_zh: 准确的运动物体分割是自动驾驶的一项重要任务。它可以为许多下游任务提供有效信息，例如碰撞避免、路径规划和静态地图构建。如何有效利用时空信息是3D LiDAR运动目标分割（LiDAR-MOS）的关键问题。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种新颖的深度神经网络，利用时空信息和 LiDAR 扫描的不同表示方式来提高 LiDAR-MOS 性能。具体来说，我们首先使用基于距离图像的双分支结构来分别处理从连续激光雷达扫描中获得的空间和时间信息，然后使用运动引导注意模块将它们组合起来。我们还通过 3D 稀疏卷积使用点细化模块来融合来自 LiDAR 距离图像和点云表示的信息，并减少对象边界上的伪影。我们在 SemanticKITTI 的 LiDAR-MOS 基准上验证了我们提出的方法的有效性。我们的方法在 LiDAR-MOS IoU 方面显着优于最先进的方法。受益于设计的从粗到精的架构，我们的方法以传感器帧速率在线运行。我们的方法的实现可以作为开源获得：https://github.com/haomo-ai/MotionSeg3D。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种高效的时空信息融合方法，用于基于LiDAR的3D移动物体分割，通过结合空间和时序信息提升分割性能。
AI.motivation: 准确的移动物体分割对自动驾驶至关重要，但如何有效利用LiDAR扫描的时空信息仍是3D LiDAR-MOS的挑战。
AI.method: 采用基于距离图像的双分支结构分别处理空间和时序信息，通过运动引导注意力模块融合，并利用3D稀疏卷积的点精炼模块减少物体边界伪影。
AI.result: 在SemanticKITTI基准测试中，该方法在LiDAR-MOS IoU指标上显著优于现有方法，且支持实时在线处理。
AI.conclusion: 所提方法通过粗到细的架构有效融合多模态时空信息，在保持实时性的同时提升了3D移动物体分割的准确性。

We verify the effectiveness of our proposed approach on the LiDAR-MOS benchmark of SemanticKITTI. Our method outperforms the state-of-the-art methods significantly in terms of LiDAR-MOS IoU. Benefiting from the devised coarse-to-fine architecture, our method operates online at sensor frame rate. The implementation of our method is available as open source at: https://github.com/haomo-ai/MotionSeg3D. 准确的运动物体分割是自动驾驶的一项重要任务。它可以为许多下游任务提供有效信息，例如碰撞避免、路径规划和静态地图构建。如何有效利用时空信息是3D LiDAR运动目标分割（LiDAR-MOS）的关键问题。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种新颖的深度神经网络，利用时空信息和 LiDAR 扫描的不同表示方式来提高 LiDAR-MOS 性能。具体来说，我们首先使用基于距离图像的双分支结构来分别处理从连续激光雷达扫描中获得的空间和时间信息，然后使用运动引导注意模块将它们组合起来。我们还通过 3D 稀疏卷积使用点细化模块来融合来自 LiDAR 距离图像和点云表示的信息，并减少对象边界上的伪影。我们在 SemanticKITTI 的 LiDAR-MOS 基准上验证了我们提出的方法的有效性。我们的方法在 LiDAR-MOS IoU 方面显着优于最先进的方法。受益于设计的从粗到精的架构，我们的方法以传感器帧速率在线运行。我们的方法的实现可以作为开源获得：https://github.com/haomo-ai/MotionSeg3D。 本文提出一种高效的时空信息融合方法，用于基于LiDAR的3D移动物体分割，通过结合空间和时序信息提升分割性能。 Jiadai Sun,Yuchao Dai,Xianjing Zhang,Jintao Xu,Rui Ai,Weihao Gu,Xieyuanli Chen cs.CV,cs.RO

authors_text: Dibya Ghosh,Anurag Ajay,Pulkit Agrawal,Sergey Levine
title: Offline RL Policies Should be Trained to be Adaptive
title_zh: 离线 RL 策略应该被训练为具有适应性
comment: ICML 2022 (long talk)
comments: ICML 2022 (long talk)
abstract: Offline RL algorithms must account for the fact that the dataset they are provided may leave many facets of the environment unknown. The most common way to approach this challenge is to employ pessimistic or conservative methods, which avoid behaviors that are too dissimilar from those in the training dataset. However, relying exclusively on conservatism has drawbacks: performance is sensitive to the exact degree of conservatism, and conservative objectives can recover highly suboptimal policies. In this work, we propose that offline RL methods should instead be adaptive in the presence of uncertainty. We show that acting optimally in offline RL in a Bayesian sense involves solving an implicit POMDP. As a result, optimal policies for offline RL must be adaptive, depending not just on the current state but rather all the transitions seen so far during evaluation.We present a model-free algorithm for approximating this optimal adaptive policy, and demonstrate the efficacy of learning such adaptive policies in offline RL benchmarks.
summary: Offline RL algorithms must account for the fact that the dataset they are provided may leave many facets of the environment unknown. The most common way to approach this challenge is to employ pessimistic or conservative methods, which avoid behaviors that are too dissimilar from those in the training dataset. However, relying exclusively on conservatism has drawbacks: performance is sensitive to the exact degree of conservatism, and conservative objectives can recover highly suboptimal policies. In this work, we propose that offline RL methods should instead be adaptive in the presence of uncertainty. We show that acting optimally in offline RL in a Bayesian sense involves solving an implicit POMDP. As a result, optimal policies for offline RL must be adaptive, depending not just on the current state but rather all the transitions seen so far during evaluation.We present a model-free algorithm for approximating this optimal adaptive policy, and demonstrate the efficacy of learning such adaptive policies in offline RL benchmarks.
abstract_zh: 离线强化学习算法必须考虑到这样一个事实：它们提供的数据集可能会让环境的许多方面变得未知。应对这一挑战的最常见方法是采用悲观或保守的方法，以避免与训练数据集中的行为过于相似的行为。然而，完全依赖保守主义也有缺点：绩效对保守主义的确切程度很敏感，而保守的目标可能会恢复高度次优的政策。在这项工作中，我们建议离线强化学习方法应该在存在不确定性的情况下进行自适应。我们证明，在贝叶斯意义上，离线 RL 中的最佳行为涉及求解隐式 POMDP。因此，离线 RL 的最优策略必须是自适应的，不仅取决于当前状态，还取决于评估期间迄今为止看到的所有转换。我们提出了一种无模型算法来逼近这种最优自适应策略，并证明了在离线 RL 基准中学习此类自适应策略的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出离线强化学习策略应通过自适应方法应对环境不确定性，而非依赖传统保守策略。
AI.motivation: 传统离线RL的保守方法存在性能敏感和策略次优问题，需在不确定性下设计更具适应性的策略。
AI.method: 将离线RL建模为隐式POMDP，提出无模型算法近似最优自适应策略，利用历史转移信息动态调整决策。
AI.result: 在多个离线RL基准测试中验证了自适应策略的有效性，优于传统保守方法。
AI.conclusion: 自适应策略能更优处理离线RL的不确定性，为该领域提供新思路与方法论。

authors_text: Hengyuan Ma,Li Zhang,Xiatian Zhu,Jianfeng Feng
title: Accelerating Score-based Generative Models with Preconditioned Diffusion Sampling
title_zh: 通过预处理扩散采样加速基于评分的生成模型
comment: ECCV 2022. Code is available at https://github.com/fudan-zvg/PDS
comments: ECCV 2022. Code is available at https://github.com/fudan-zvg/PDS
abstract: Score-based generative models (SGMs) have recently emerged as a promising class of generative models. However, a fundamental limitation is that their inference is very slow due to a need for many (e.g., 2000) iterations of sequential computations. An intuitive acceleration method is to reduce the sampling iterations which however causes severe performance degradation. We investigate this problem by viewing the diffusion sampling process as a Metropolis adjusted Langevin algorithm, which helps reveal the underlying cause to be ill-conditioned curvature. Under this insight, we propose a model-agnostic preconditioned diffusion sampling (PDS) method that leverages matrix preconditioning to alleviate the aforementioned problem. Crucially, PDS is proven theoretically to converge to the original target distribution of a SGM, no need for retraining. Extensive experiments on three image datasets with a variety of resolutions and diversity validate that PDS consistently accelerates off-the-shelf SGMs whilst maintaining the synthesis quality. In particular, PDS can accelerate by up to 29x on more challenging high resolution (1024x1024) image generation.
summary: Score-based generative models (SGMs) have recently emerged as a promising class of generative models. However, a fundamental limitation is that their inference is very slow due to a need for many (e.g., 2000) iterations of sequential computations. An intuitive acceleration method is to reduce the sampling iterations which however causes severe performance degradation. We investigate this problem by viewing the diffusion sampling process as a Metropolis adjusted Langevin algorithm, which helps reveal the underlying cause to be ill-conditioned curvature. Under this insight, we propose a model-agnostic preconditioned diffusion sampling (PDS) method that leverages matrix preconditioning to alleviate the aforementioned problem. Crucially, PDS is proven theoretically to converge to the original target distribution of a SGM, no need for retraining. Extensive experiments on three image datasets with a variety of resolutions and diversity validate that PDS consistently accelerates off-the-shelf SGMs whilst maintaining the synthesis quality. In particular, PDS can accelerate by up to 29x on more challenging high resolution (1024x1024) image generation.
abstract_zh: 基于分数的生成模型（SGM）最近成为一类有前途的生成模型。然而，一个根本的限制是，由于需要多次（例如 2000 次）顺序计算迭代，它们的推理速度非常慢。一种直观的加速方法是减少采样迭代，但这会导致严重的性能下降。我们通过将扩散采样过程视为 Metropolis 调整的 Langevin 算法来研究这个问题，这有助于揭示病态曲率的根本原因。根据这一见解，我们提出了一种与模型无关的预处理扩散采样（PDS）方法，该方法利用矩阵预处理来缓解上述问题。至关重要的是，PDS 在理论上被证明可以收敛到 SGM 的原始目标分布，无需重新训练。对具有各种分辨率和多样性的三个图像数据集进行的广泛实验验证了 PDS 能够持续加速现成的 SGM，同时保持合成质量。特别是，PDS 可以将更具挑战性的高分辨率 (1024x1024) 图像生成速度提高高达 29 倍。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为预条件扩散采样（PDS）的方法，用于加速基于分数的生成模型（SGMs）的推理过程。
AI.motivation: 基于分数的生成模型（SGMs）在生成高质量数据方面表现出色，但其推理过程需要大量迭代计算，导致速度缓慢。直接减少采样迭代次数会显著降低生成质量，因此需要更有效的加速方法。
AI.method: 将扩散采样过程视为Metropolis调整的朗之万算法，通过矩阵预处理技术解决曲率病态问题，提出无需重新训练即可收敛到原始目标分布的模型无关预条件扩散采样（PDS）方法。
AI.result: 在三个不同分辨率和多样性的图像数据集上，PDS方法在保持生成质量的同时，将基于分数的生成模型的推理速度提升了最高达29倍（尤其在1024x1024高分辨率图像生成中表现显著）。
AI.conclusion: PDS方法通过理论保证和实验验证，为加速基于分数的生成模型提供了高效且通用的解决方案，无需修改原有模型结构或重新训练。

authors_text: Lei Xiao,Salah Nouri,Joel Hegland,Alberto Garcia Garcia,Douglas Lanman
title: NeuralPassthrough: Learned Real-Time View Synthesis for VR
title_zh: NeuralPassthrough：学习 VR 实时视图合成
comment: 9 pages, 12 figures
comments: 9 pages, 12 figures
abstract: Virtual reality (VR) headsets provide an immersive, stereoscopic visual experience, but at the cost of blocking users from directly observing their physical environment. Passthrough techniques are intended to address this limitation by leveraging outward-facing cameras to reconstruct the images that would otherwise be seen by the user without the headset. This is inherently a real-time view synthesis challenge, since passthrough cameras cannot be physically co-located with the eyes. Existing passthrough techniques suffer from distracting reconstruction artifacts, largely due to the lack of accurate depth information (especially for near-field and disoccluded objects), and also exhibit limited image quality (e.g., being low resolution and monochromatic). In this paper, we propose the first learned passthrough method and assess its performance using a custom VR headset that contains a stereo pair of RGB cameras. Through both simulations and experiments, we demonstrate that our learned passthrough method delivers superior image quality compared to state-of-the-art methods, while meeting strict VR requirements for real-time, perspective-correct stereoscopic view synthesis over a wide field of view for desktop-connected headsets.
summary: Virtual reality (VR) headsets provide an immersive, stereoscopic visual experience, but at the cost of blocking users from directly observing their physical environment. Passthrough techniques are intended to address this limitation by leveraging outward-facing cameras to reconstruct the images that would otherwise be seen by the user without the headset. This is inherently a real-time view synthesis challenge, since passthrough cameras cannot be physically co-located with the eyes. Existing passthrough techniques suffer from distracting reconstruction artifacts, largely due to the lack of accurate depth information (especially for near-field and disoccluded objects), and also exhibit limited image quality (e.g., being low resolution and monochromatic). In this paper, we propose the first learned passthrough method and assess its performance using a custom VR headset that contains a stereo pair of RGB cameras. Through both simulations and experiments, we demonstrate that our learned passthrough method delivers superior image quality compared to state-of-the-art methods, while meeting strict VR requirements for real-time, perspective-correct stereoscopic view synthesis over a wide field of view for desktop-connected headsets.
abstract_zh: 虚拟现实 (VR) 耳机提供身临其境的立体视觉体验，但代价是阻止用户直接观察其物理环境。直通技术旨在通过利用外置摄像头来重建用户在没有耳机的情况下看到的图像，从而解决这一限制。这本质上是实时视图合成的挑战，因为直通相机无法与眼睛物理位于同一位置。现有的直通技术存在分散注意力的重建伪影，这主要是由于缺乏准确的深度信息（特别是对于近场和被遮挡的物体），并且还表现出有限的图像质量（例如，低分辨率和单色）。在本文中，我们提出了第一个学习直通方法，并使用包含一对立体 RGB 摄像头的定制 VR 耳机评估其性能。通过模拟和实验，我们证明，与最先进的方法相比，我们学习的直通方法可提供卓越的图像质量，同时满足桌面连接耳机在宽视场上实时、透视正确的立体视图合成的严格 VR 要求。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于学习的实时视图合成方法NeuralPassthrough，用于解决VR头显的透视问题。
AI.motivation: 现有Passthrough技术因缺乏准确深度信息导致重建伪影多，且图像质量差（低分辨率、单色）。
AI.method: 设计首个学习型Passthrough方法，结合定制VR头显的双RGB摄像头，通过模拟与实验验证实时性与立体视觉合成能力。
AI.result: 在图像质量与实时性上优于现有方法，满足宽视场角下桌面连接头显的透视校正需求。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效提升VR环境感知质量，为实时立体视图合成提供新解决方案。

search_text: NeuralPassthrough: Learned Real-Time View Synthesis for VR NeuralPassthrough：学习 VR 实时视图合成 Virtual reality (VR) headsets provide an immersive, stereoscopic visual experience, but at the cost of blocking users from directly observing their physical environment. Passthrough techniques are intended to address this limitation by leveraging outward-facing cameras to reconstruct the images that would otherwise be seen by the user without the headset. This is inherently a real-time view synthesis challenge, since passthrough cameras cannot be physically co-located with the eyes. Existing passthrough techniques suffer from distracting reconstruction artifacts, largely due to the lack of accurate depth information (especially for near-field and disoccluded objects), and also exhibit limited image quality (e.g., being low resolution and monochromatic). In this paper, we propose the first learned passthrough method and assess its performance using a custom VR headset that contains a stereo pair of RGB cameras. Through both simulations and experiments, we demonstrate that our learned passthrough method delivers superior image quality compared to state-of-the-art methods, while meeting strict VR requirements for real-time, perspective-correct stereoscopic view synthesis over a wide field of view for desktop-connected headsets. 虚拟现实 (VR) 耳机提供身临其境的立体视觉体验，但代价是阻止用户直接观察其物理环境。直通技术旨在通过利用外置摄像头来重建用户在没有耳机的情况下看到的图像，从而解决这一限制。这本质上是实时视图合成的挑战，因为直通相机无法与眼睛物理位于同一位置。现有的直通技术存在分散注意力的重建伪影，这主要是由于缺乏准确的深度信息（特别是对于近场和被遮挡的物体），并且还表现出有限的图像质量（例如，低分辨率和单色）。在本文中，我们提出了第一个学习直通方法，并使用包含一对立体 RGB 摄像头的定制 VR 耳机评估其性能。通过模拟和实验，我们证明，与最先进的方法相比，我们学习的直通方法可提供卓越的图像质量，同时满足桌面连接耳机在宽视场上实时、透视正确的立体视图合成的严格 VR 要求。 本文提出一种基于学习的实时视图合成方法NeuralPassthrough，用于解决VR头显的透视问题。 Lei Xiao,Salah Nouri,Joel Hegland,Alberto Garcia Garcia,Douglas Lanman cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Jialu Li,Hao Tan,Mohit Bansal
title: CLEAR: Improving Vision-Language Navigation with Cross-Lingual, Environment-Agnostic Representations
title_zh: CLEAR：通过跨语言、与环境无关的表示来改进视觉语言导航
comment: NAACL 2022 Findings (18 pages)
comments: NAACL 2022 Findings (18 pages)
abstract: Vision-and-Language Navigation (VLN) tasks require an agent to navigate through the environment based on language instructions. In this paper, we aim to solve two key challenges in this task: utilizing multilingual instructions for improved instruction-path grounding and navigating through new environments that are unseen during training. To address these challenges, we propose CLEAR: Cross-Lingual and Environment-Agnostic Representations. First, our agent learns a shared and visually-aligned cross-lingual language representation for the three languages (English, Hindi and Telugu) in the Room-Across-Room dataset. Our language representation learning is guided by text pairs that are aligned by visual information. Second, our agent learns an environment-agnostic visual representation by maximizing the similarity between semantically-aligned image pairs (with constraints on object-matching) from different environments. Our environment agnostic visual representation can mitigate the environment bias induced by low-level visual information. Empirically, on the Room-Across-Room dataset, we show that our multilingual agent gets large improvements in all metrics over the strong baseline model when generalizing to unseen environments with the cross-lingual language representation and the environment-agnostic visual representation. Furthermore, we show that our learned language and visual representations can be successfully transferred to the Room-to-Room and Cooperative Vision-and-Dialogue Navigation task, and present detailed qualitative and quantitative generalization and grounding analysis. Our code is available at https://github.com/jialuli-luka/CLEAR

In this paper, we aim to solve two key challenges in this task: utilizing multilingual instructions for improved instruction-path grounding and navigating through new environments that are unseen during training. To address these challenges, we propose CLEAR: Cross-Lingual and Environment-Agnostic Representations. First, our agent learns a shared and visually-aligned cross-lingual language representation for the three languages (English, Hindi and Telugu) in the Room-Across-Room dataset. Our language representation learning is guided by text pairs that are aligned by visual information. Second, our agent learns an environment-agnostic visual representation by maximizing the similarity between semantically-aligned image pairs (with constraints on object-matching) from different environments. Our environment agnostic visual representation can mitigate the environment bias induced by low-level visual information. Empirically, on the Room-Across-Room dataset, we show that our multilingual agent gets large improvements in all metrics over the strong baseline model when generalizing to unseen environments with the cross-lingual language representation and the environment-agnostic visual representation. Furthermore, we show that our learned language and visual representations can be successfully transferred to the Room-to-Room and Cooperative Vision-and-Dialogue Navigation task, and present detailed qualitative and quantitative generalization and grounding analysis. Our code is available at https://github.com/jialuli-luka/CLEAR
abstract_zh: Vision-and-Language Navigation (VLN) tasks require an agent to navigate through the environment based on language instructions.在本文中，我们的目标是解决这项任务中的两个关键挑战：利用多语言指令来改进指令路径基础，以及在训练过程中看不见的新环境中导航。 To address these challenges, we propose CLEAR: Cross-Lingual and Environment-Agnostic Representations.首先，我们的代理在 Room-Across-Room 数据集中学习三种语言（英语、印地语和泰卢固语）的共享且视觉对齐的跨语言语言表示。 Our language representation learning is guided by text pairs that are aligned by visual information.其次，我们的代理通过最大化来自不同环境的语义对齐图像对（具有对象匹配的限制）之间的相似性来学习与环境无关的视觉表示。 Our environment agnostic visual representation can mitigate the environment bias induced by low-level visual information.根据经验，在 Room-Across-Room 数据集上，我们表明，当使用跨语言语言表示和与环境无关的视觉表示泛化到不可见的环境时，我们的多语言代理在所有指标上都比强基线模型有了很大的改进。此外，我们表明，我们学习的语言和视觉表示可以成功地转移到房间到房间和合作视觉与对话导航任务，并提出详细的定性和定量概括和基础分析。 Our code is available at https://github.com/jialuli-luka/CLEAR

AI.tldr: 提出CLEAR方法，通过跨语言和环境无关的表示提升视觉-语言导航任务的性能。
AI.motivation: 现有视觉-语言导航方法在处理多语言指令和新环境时存在泛化能力不足的问题，需解决跨语言指令对齐与环境偏差的挑战。
AI.method: 1. 学习跨语言共享的视觉对齐语言表示（英语/印地语/泰卢固语）；2. 通过语义对齐图像对（带对象匹配约束）学习环境无关视觉表示，消除环境偏差。
AI.result: 在Room-Across-Room数据集上显著优于基线模型，且表示可迁移至Room-to-Room和协作导航任务，具备良好的泛化能力。
AI.conclusion: CLEAR方法通过解耦语言与视觉表示，有效提升了多语言导航和跨环境泛化能力，为VLN研究提供了新方向。

authors_text: Jae Soon Baik,In Young Yoon,Jun Won Choi
title: ST-CoNAL: Consistency-Based Acquisition Criterion Using Temporal Self-Ensemble for Active Learning
title_zh: ST-CoNAL：使用时间自集成进行主动学习的基于一致性的采集标准
abstract: Modern deep learning has achieved great success in various fields. However, it requires the labeling of huge amounts of data, which is expensive and labor-intensive. Active learning (AL), which identifies the most informative samples to be labeled, is becoming increasingly important to maximize the efficiency of the training process. The existing AL methods mostly use only a single final fixed model for acquiring the samples to be labeled. This strategy may not be good enough in that the structural uncertainty of a model for given training data is not considered to acquire the samples. In this study, we propose a novel acquisition criterion based on temporal self-ensemble generated by conventional stochastic gradient descent (SGD) optimization. These self-ensemble models are obtained by capturing the intermediate network weights obtained through SGD iterations. Our acquisition function relies on a consistency measure between the student and teacher models. The student models are given a fixed number of temporal self-ensemble models, and the teacher model is constructed by averaging the weights of the student models. Using the proposed acquisition criterion, we present an AL algorithm, namely student-teacher consistency-based AL (ST-CoNAL). Experiments conducted for image classification tasks on CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, Caltech-256, and Tiny ImageNet datasets demonstrate that the proposed ST-CoNAL achieves significantly better performance than the existing acquisition methods. Furthermore, extensive experiments show the robustness and effectiveness of our methods.
summary: Modern deep learning has achieved great success in various fields. However, it requires the labeling of huge amounts of data, which is expensive and labor-intensive. Active learning (AL), which identifies the most informative samples to be labeled, is becoming increasingly important to maximize the efficiency of the training process. The existing AL methods mostly use only a single final fixed model for acquiring the samples to be labeled. This strategy may not be good enough in that the structural uncertainty of a model for given training data is not considered to acquire the samples. In this study, we propose a novel acquisition criterion based on temporal self-ensemble generated by conventional stochastic gradient descent (SGD) optimization. These self-ensemble models are obtained by capturing the intermediate network weights obtained through SGD iterations. Our acquisition function relies on a consistency measure between the student and teacher models. The student models are given a fixed number of temporal self-ensemble models, and the teacher model is constructed by averaging the weights of the student models. Using the proposed acquisition criterion, we present an AL algorithm, namely student-teacher consistency-based AL (ST-CoNAL). Experiments conducted for image classification tasks on CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, Caltech-256, and Tiny ImageNet datasets demonstrate that the proposed ST-CoNAL achieves significantly better performance than the existing acquisition methods. Furthermore, extensive experiments show the robustness and effectiveness of our methods.
abstract_zh: 现代深度学习在各个领域都取得了巨大的成功。然而，它需要对大量数据进行标记，这是昂贵且劳动密集型的。主动学习（AL）可以识别信息量最大的待标记样本，对于最大限度地提高训练过程的效率变得越来越重要。现有的AL方法大多仅使用单一的最终固定模型来获取待标记的样本。该策略可能不够好，因为在获取样本时没有考虑给定训练数据的模型的结构不确定性。在本研究中，我们提出了一种基于传统随机梯度下降（SGD）优化生成的时间自系综的新颖采集标准。这些自集成模型是通过捕获通过 SGD 迭代获得的中间网络权重而获得的。我们的习得功能依赖于学生模型和教师模型之间的一致性度量。给学生模型固定数量的时间自集成模型，并通过平均学生模型的权重来构建教师模型。使用所提出的获取标准，我们提出了一种 AL 算法，即基于学生-教师一致性的 AL (ST-CoNAL)。在 CIFAR-10、CIFAR-100、Caltech-256 和 Tiny ImageNet 数据集上进行的图像分类任务实验表明，所提出的 ST-CoNAL 比现有的采集方法具有明显更好的性能。此外，大量的实验表明了我们方法的稳健性和有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于时间自集成的主动学习方法ST-CoNAL，通过一致性度量选择最信息量的样本进行标注。
AI.motivation: 现有主动学习方法仅依赖单一固定模型，未能考虑模型对训练数据的结构不确定性，导致样本选择效率不足。
AI.method: 利用SGD迭代过程中的中间网络权重生成时间自集成模型，通过学生模型与教师模型（权重平均后的模型）之间的一致性度量构建新的样本选择准则。
AI.result: 在CIFAR-10、CIFAR-100等数据集上的图像分类任务实验表明，ST-CoNAL显著优于现有方法，且具有鲁棒性和有效性。
AI.conclusion: ST-CoNAL通过考虑模型结构不确定性，有效提升了主动学习的样本选择性能，验证了方法的可行性与优势。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于时间自集成的主动学习方法ST-CoNAL，通过一致性度量选择最信息量的样本进行标注。
ai_content.motivation: 现有主动学习方法仅依赖单一固定模型，未能考虑模型对训练数据的结构不确定性，导致样本选择效率不足。
ai_content.method: 利用SGD迭代过程中的中间网络权重生成时间自集成模型，通过学生模型与教师模型（权重平均后的模型）之间的一致性度量构建新的样本选择准则。
ai_content.result: 在CIFAR-10、CIFAR-100等数据集上的图像分类任务实验表明，ST-CoNAL显著优于现有方法，且具有鲁棒性和有效性。
ai_content.conclusion: ST-CoNAL通过考虑模型结构不确定性，有效提升了主动学习的样本选择性能，验证了方法的可行性与优势。

search_text: ST-CoNAL: Consistency-Based Acquisition Criterion Using Temporal Self-Ensemble for Active Learning ST-CoNAL：使用时间自集成进行主动学习的基于一致性的采集标准 Modern deep learning has achieved great success in various fields. However, it requires the labeling of huge amounts of data, which is expensive and labor-intensive. Active learning (AL), which identifies the most informative samples to be labeled, is becoming increasingly important to maximize the efficiency of the training process. The existing AL methods mostly use only a single final fixed model for acquiring the samples to be labeled. This strategy may not be good enough in that the structural uncertainty of a model for given training data is not considered to acquire the samples. In this study, we propose a novel acquisition criterion based on temporal self-ensemble generated by conventional stochastic gradient descent (SGD) optimization. These self-ensemble models are obtained by capturing the intermediate network weights obtained through SGD iterations. Our acquisition function relies on a consistency measure between the student and teacher models. The student models are given a fixed number of temporal self-ensemble models, and the teacher model is constructed by averaging the weights of the student models. Using the proposed acquisition criterion, we present an AL algorithm, namely student-teacher consistency-based AL (ST-CoNAL). Experiments conducted for image classification tasks on CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, Caltech-256, and Tiny ImageNet datasets demonstrate that the proposed ST-CoNAL achieves significantly better performance than the existing acquisition methods. Furthermore, extensive experiments show the robustness and effectiveness of our methods. 现代深度学习在各个领域都取得了巨大的成功。然而，它需要对大量数据进行标记，这是昂贵且劳动密集型的。主动学习（AL）可以识别信息量最大的待标记样本，对于最大限度地提高训练过程的效率变得越来越重要。现有的AL方法大多仅使用单一的最终固定模型来获取待标记的样本。该策略可能不够好，因为在获取样本时没有考虑给定训练数据的模型的结构不确定性。在本研究中，我们提出了一种基于传统随机梯度下降（SGD）优化生成的时间自系综的新颖采集标准。这些自集成模型是通过捕获通过 SGD 迭代获得的中间网络权重而获得的。我们的习得功能依赖于学生模型和教师模型之间的一致性度量。给学生模型固定数量的时间自集成模型，并通过平均学生模型的权重来构建教师模型。使用所提出的获取标准，我们提出了一种 AL 算法，即基于学生-教师一致性的 AL (ST-CoNAL)。在 CIFAR-10、CIFAR-100、Caltech-256 和 Tiny ImageNet 数据集上进行的图像分类任务实验表明，所提出的 ST-CoNAL 比现有的采集方法具有明显更好的性能。此外，大量的实验表明了我们方法的稳健性和有效性。 本文提出了一种基于时间自集成的主动学习方法ST-CoNAL，通过一致性度量选择最信息量的样本进行标注。 Jae Soon Baik,In Young Yoon,Jun Won Choi cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Huawei Lin,Haozhe Liu,Qiufu Li,Linlin Shen
title: Activation Template Matching Loss for Explainable Face Recognition
title_zh: 可解释人脸识别的激活模板匹配损失
comment: 13 pages, 7 figures, 5 tables
comments: 13 pages, 7 figures, 5 tables
abstract: Can we construct an explainable face recognition network able to learn a facial part-based feature like eyes, nose, mouth and so forth, without any manual annotation or additionalsion datasets? In this paper, we propose a generic Explainable Channel Loss (ECLoss) to construct an explainable face recognition network. The explainable network trained with ECLoss can easily learn the facial part-based representation on the target convolutional layer, where an individual channel can detect a certain face part. Our experiments on dozens of datasets show that ECLoss achieves superior explainability metrics, and at the same time improves the performance of face verification without face alignment. In addition, our visualization results also illustrate the effectiveness of the proposed ECLoss.
summary: Can we construct an explainable face recognition network able to learn a facial part-based feature like eyes, nose, mouth and so forth, without any manual annotation or additionalsion datasets? In this paper, we propose a generic Explainable Channel Loss (ECLoss) to construct an explainable face recognition network. The explainable network trained with ECLoss can easily learn the facial part-based representation on the target convolutional layer, where an individual channel can detect a certain face part. Our experiments on dozens of datasets show that ECLoss achieves superior explainability metrics, and at the same time improves the performance of face verification without face alignment. In addition, our visualization results also illustrate the effectiveness of the proposed ECLoss.
abstract_zh: 我们能否构建一个可解释的人脸识别网络，能够学习基于面部部位的特征，如眼睛、鼻子、嘴巴等，而无需任何手动注释或附加数据集？ In this paper, we propose a generic Explainable Channel Loss (ECLoss) to construct an explainable face recognition network.使用 ELoss 训练的可解释网络可以轻松学习目标卷积层上基于面部部位的表示，其中单个通道可以检测某个面部部位。我们对数十个数据集的实验表明，ECLoss 实现了卓越的可解释性指标，同时提高了无需人脸对齐的人脸验证的性能。 In addition, our visualization results also illustrate the effectiveness of the proposed ECLoss.

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种无需手动标注的可解释人脸识别网络，通过设计Explainable Channel Loss（ECLoss）学习面部局部特征。
AI.motivation: 传统人脸识别方法依赖手动标注或额外数据集来提取面部局部特征，而本文旨在构建无需人工干预的可解释模型。
AI.method: 设计ECLoss损失函数，在目标卷积层引导网络学习面部局部特征表示，使每个通道对应特定面部部件（如眼睛、鼻子等）。
AI.result: 在多个数据集上验证了ECLoss的优越可解释性指标，同时提升无对齐人脸识别性能，可视化结果证实了方法有效性。
AI.conclusion: ECLoss成功实现了无需标注的可解释人脸识别，为模型可解释性研究提供了新思路和有效方法。
